target.title: fix(mcp): bound tools/list during catalog discovery
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the original hardening reused `connectionTimeoutMs` as the post-connect MCP `tools/list` request timeout, but that field is documented as connection-only and can hide slow-but-valid catalogs after upgrade.
- Solution: add an explicit optional `toolsListTimeoutMs` for post-connect MCP catalog listing, while preserving the previous SDK/default request-timeout behavior when it is not configured.
- What changed: config type/schema/help/labels/docs now expose `toolsListTimeoutMs`; MCP transport resolution threads it through; catalog discovery passes `{ timeout }` to `client.listTools(...)` only when the new field is set.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no MCP tool execution behavior, auth, transport selection, provider/tool inventory policy, pagination semantics, or default catalog request timeout behavior.

## Motivation

- Related #64411: MCP/plugin failures should degrade predictably instead of blocking the main path.
- Related #82842 covers startup/connect hardening; this PR covers the adjacent post-connect `tools/list` catalog phase without changing the connection-timeout contract.
- Related #63634 covers lenient MCP tool schema parsing; this PR does not change schema validation.
- This is intentionally a narrow compatibility-preserving availability hardening slice; #64411 remains broader follow-up scope.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #: N/A
- Related #64411
- Related #82842
- Related #63634
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: slow-but-valid bundled MCP catalogs remain visible by default even with a low `connectionTimeoutMs`, while an explicitly configured `toolsListTimeoutMs` can bound and degrade a hung post-connect `tools/list` request.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout at `d15d78d91b59919fb4368e2aa5cf7d599d24a28a` on Linux 6.17.0-14-generic x86_64, Node.js v22.22.1.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-slow-list-red-client.mjs
node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-hanging-list-explicit-client.mjs
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts
node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/cli/mcp.md docs/plugins/bundles.md
node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
```

- Evidence after fix:

Terminal capture from this branch, copied live output:

```text
$ node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-slow-list-red-client.mjs
{
  "elapsedMs": 558,
  "toolNames": [
    "slow_tool"
  ],
  "serverCount": 1,
  "serverLog": [
    "recv initialize",
    "send 0 response",
    "recv notifications/initialized",
    "delay tools/list 450",
    "recv tools/list",
    "send 1 response"
  ]
}

$ node --import tsx /tmp/pr85063-hanging-list-explicit-client.mjs
[bundle-mcp] failed to start server "hangingList" (/root/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin/node /tmp/pr85063-hanging-list-explicit-server.mjs): McpError: MCP error -32001: Request timed out
{
  "elapsedMs": 505,
  "toolNames": [],
  "serverCount": 0,
  "serverLog": [
    "recv initialize",
    "send 0 response",
    "recv notifications/initialized",
    "recv tools/list",
    "hang tools/list",
    "recv notifications/cancelled"
  ]
}
```

- Observed result after fix: without `toolsListTimeoutMs`, the slow 450 ms `tools/list` response produced `slow_tool` even though the fake server config used `connectionTimeoutMs: 250`; with `toolsListTimeoutMs: 250`, the hanging list request was cancelled and catalog discovery resolved degraded/empty in 505 ms.
- What was not tested: live third-party MCP servers, live SSE/streamable HTTP servers, malformed pagination/page-limit behavior, and full repository test suite. HTTP/SSE config threading is covered by resolver tests; source-runtime proof uses stdio.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before the compatibility fix, the same slow-list source-runtime repro with `connectionTimeoutMs: 250` and no `toolsListTimeoutMs` returned `toolNames: []`, `serverCount: 0`, and logged an SDK request timeout before the fake server's delayed `tools/list` response.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: the earlier hardening applied the existing `connectionTimeoutMs` to `client.listTools(...)` after connection, broadening a documented connection-only field into a catalog-list request budget.
- Missing detection / guardrail: coverage had a hanging `tools/list` case, but not the compatibility case where a server connects quickly and returns a slow-but-valid catalog under the SDK default request timeout.
- Contributing context (if known): the existing catalog path already catches per-server listing failures and degrades; the missing piece was separating the post-connect list budget from the connection budget.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts`, `src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: default catalog discovery calls `client.listTools(params)` without a timeout override, an explicit `toolsListTimeoutMs` bounds a hanging list request, and resolver output carries the new optional field for stdio and HTTP transports.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: it uses the real session MCP runtime and stdio transport boundary for behavior, plus resolver tests for config threading, while keeping fake servers deterministic and local.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): existing startup/connect tests covered only connection failure/degrade behavior, not post-connect list compatibility.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A; this PR adds regression coverage.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds optional `mcp.servers.<name>.toolsListTimeoutMs` for MCP catalog listing. When omitted, OpenClaw preserves the SDK/default request-timeout behavior for `tools/list`; when set, a slow or hung post-connect catalog request is skipped/degraded after that timeout.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Default:
getCatalog() -> connectWithTimeout(connectionTimeoutMs) -> client.listTools(params) -> SDK/default request timeout remains in effect

Opt-in list timeout:
getCatalog() -> connectWithTimeout(connectionTimeoutMs) -> client.listTools(params, { timeout: toolsListTimeoutMs }) -> per-server failure is caught -> partial/empty catalog resolves
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No; this only bounds an existing catalog-listing request and does not execute MCP tools.
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux 6.17.0-14-generic x86_64
- Runtime/container: local source checkout, Node.js v22.22.1
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): bundled MCP stdio transport for source-runtime proof; resolver tests cover stdio and HTTP config threading
- Relevant config (redacted): fake slow-list MCP server with `connectionTimeoutMs: 250` and no `toolsListTimeoutMs`; fake hanging MCP server with `connectionTimeoutMs: 30000` and `toolsListTimeoutMs: 250`

### Steps

1. Run the two source-runtime smoke commands shown in `## Real behavior proof`.
2. Run the focused runtime/config regression tests:
   ```bash
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts src/agents/mcp-transport-config.test.ts
   ```
3. Run related schema/docs/type checks:
   ```bash
   node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts
   node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check docs/cli/mcp.md docs/plugins/bundles.md
   node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
   node --import tsx scripts/generate-config-doc-baseline.ts --check
   node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
   ```

### Expected

- A slow-but-valid `tools/list` response remains visible by default when no list timeout is configured.
- A hanging `tools/list` request degrades quickly when `toolsListTimeoutMs` is configured.
- `connectionTimeoutMs` remains a connection-only budget.

### Actual

- Slow-list source-runtime smoke resolved in 544 ms with `toolNames: ["slow_tool"]` and `serverCount: 1`.
- Explicit-timeout source-runtime smoke resolved in 379 ms with `toolNames: []`, `serverCount: 0`, and `notifications/cancelled` in the fake server log.
- Focused MCP/config tests passed: 4 files / 48 tests.
- Config/schema tests passed: 3 files / 76 tests.
- Docs format, generated config schema checks, generated config baseline check, and core test-project typecheck passed.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: corrected RED behavior before the fix, slow-but-valid default behavior after the fix, explicit list-timeout degradation after the fix, resolver threading for stdio and HTTP, schema/help/docs/generated-baseline alignment, and core test-project typecheck.
- Edge cases checked: non-positive `toolsListTimeoutMs` is ignored like an absent optional timeout, `connectionTimeoutMs` still defaults to 30 seconds when missing, low `connectionTimeoutMs` no longer shortens `tools/list` by default, and fake server cleanup/disposal paths complete.
- What you did **not** verify: live third-party MCP servers, live non-stdio transports, malformed pagination/page-limit behavior, and full repository test suite.

## Review Conversations

- [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

The existing compatibility finding is addressed by this code update and proof; no manual bot conversation has been resolved in the GitHub UI.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes; this adds optional `mcp.servers.<name>.toolsListTimeoutMs`.
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A. Operators who want a shorter post-connect catalog-listing budget can opt in by setting `toolsListTimeoutMs` per MCP server.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: a too-low `toolsListTimeoutMs` can still skip a slow-but-valid catalog.
  - Mitigation: the field is opt-in, and the default leaves the SDK/default request timeout in place for compatibility.
- Risk: hung catalogs still wait for the SDK/default request timeout unless the new field is configured.
  - Mitigation: this preserves existing behavior by default and gives operators an explicit list-timeout knob without changing the connection-timeout contract.
- Risk: generated config docs/baseline can drift when adding a new config field.
  - Mitigation: `generate-base-config-schema --check` and `generate-config-doc-baseline --check` passed after regenerating the baseline hash.

target.title: fix(auth): add --token flag to paste-token and surface validTypes in invalid_type warning
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #63042. Improves non-interactive auth setup and debuggability of auth profile load errors.

## Changes

- Adds `openclaw models auth paste-token --token <value>` for trusted scripted setup.
- Keeps the existing interactive `paste-token` prompt when `--token` is omitted.
- Rejects empty `--token` values before persisting credentials.
- Validates Anthropic setup-token values before persisting them.
- Includes accepted `validTypes` in invalid auth-profile warning logs.
- Documents `--token` and its argv exposure tradeoff in `docs/cli/models.md`.
- Adds an Unreleased changelog entry for the user-facing CLI fix.

## Security note

`--token <value>` is a convenience path for trusted shells and automation, but inline secrets can be exposed through shell history, command echoing, CI logs, or process-list tools. The CLI help and docs now say this explicitly and recommend the interactive prompt when possible.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `models auth paste-token --token <value>` now runs through the real CLI without opening the interactive token prompt, updates the auth profile, and prints the normal config/profile update output.

Real environment tested: Local checkout of PR head `57874b9b64034f7b49719067b03f29683c909b33` on 2026-05-08, running the real OpenClaw CLI through `corepack pnpm openclaw` with an isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```console
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home \
  OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw \
  OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw_pr63050_home/.openclaw/openclaw.json \
  corepack pnpm openclaw models auth paste-token \
    --provider openai \
    --profile-id proof:manual \
    --token proof-redacted-token
```

Evidence after fix: Copied terminal output from the real CLI command above:

```console
Updated $OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/openclaw.json
Auth profile: proof:manual (openai/token)
```

Observed result after fix: The command completed successfully without an interactive prompt and wrote the requested `proof:manual` OpenAI token auth profile in the isolated OpenClaw home.

What was not tested: A real provider token was not used; the changed behavior under test is CLI non-interactive token ingestion and profile persistence, not provider-side authentication.

## Test plan

- [x] `pnpm test src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts`
- [x] `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/cli/models-cli.ts src/commands/models/auth.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/persisted.ts src/cli/models-cli.test.ts src/commands/models/auth.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.ensureauthprofilestore.test.ts docs/cli/models.md CHANGELOG.md`
- [x] `git diff --check`
- [x] Real CLI proof above shows `models auth paste-token --token` completes without an interactive prompt.

target.title: fix(cron): warn on main heartbeat handoff ghost runs
target.body:
Fixes #63106.

## Summary
- add a scoped warning for fast ok main-session systemEvent jobs using wakeMode=next-heartbeat
- annotate cron run logs with possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run for that handoff path
- add cron.ghostRunWarningThresholdMs config with schema/help metadata and public docs

## Tests
- node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts
- node --no-maglev node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.config.ts src/config/zod-schema.cron-retention.test.ts src/config/schema.base.generated.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts src/cron/run-log.test.ts
- node --import tsx scripts/generate-base-config-schema.ts --check
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig tsconfig.oxlint.core.json src/gateway/server-cron.ts src/gateway/server-cron.test.ts src/config/types.cron.ts src/config/zod-schema.ts src/config/schema.help.ts src/config/schema.labels.ts src/cron/run-log.ts
- git diff --check

Latest PR-head verification also passed the full GitHub check set, with only neutral/skipped CodeQL remaining. Re-review retry note: the prior ClawSweeper run timed out before evaluating the latest proof/docs.

Note: local tsgo checks currently fail before this change on unrelated dependency/type issues (missing typebox and existing model compat errors).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Verified that a fast successful main-session `systemEvent` cron handoff using `wakeMode: "next-heartbeat"` writes the `possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run` warning into the cron run log.
- Real environment tested: Local macOS checkout on this PR head, using the real `buildGatewayCronService` and real cron run-log reader with an isolated temporary `OPENCLAW_HOME`. This did not touch the real user cron store.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_HOME=$(mktemp -d /tmp/openclaw-ghost-proof.XXXXXX) node --import tsx -e '... buildGatewayCronService(...); add main next-heartbeat systemEvent job; force run; readCronRunLogEntries(...)'
```

- Evidence after fix: Redacted terminal output from the isolated run-log proof:

```json
{
  "home": "/tmp/openclaw-ghost-proof.redacted",
  "jobId": "redacted-job-id",
  "logPath": "/tmp/openclaw-ghost-proof.redacted/runs/redacted-job-id.jsonl",
  "latest": {
    "action": "finished",
    "status": "ok",
    "summary": "proof handoff",
    "runId": "cron:redacted-job-id:redacted-start-ms",
    "durationMs": 7,
    "deliveryStatus": "not-requested",
    "warnings": [
      "possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run"
    ]
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The forced main-session `next-heartbeat` handoff completed successfully and the persisted run-log entry contains `warnings: ["possible-main-next-heartbeat-ghost-run"]`.
- Public docs proof: The branch now documents `cron.ghostRunWarningThresholdMs` in `docs/cli/cron.md` and `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, including the default `50` ms threshold and `0` to disable.
- Additional gateway context: The same local machine also had a real running gateway reachable before this isolated proof (`openclaw gateway status` reported LaunchAgent loaded, runtime active, and connectivity probe ok).
- Focused regression test: `server-cron.test.ts > buildGatewayCronService > warns and annotates fast main next-heartbeat systemEvent handoffs`.
- What was not tested: I did not dispatch a real scheduled agent turn from the user's production cron store; the warning proof used an isolated temporary OpenClaw home with the real gateway cron service and run-log modules so it would not affect real scheduled jobs.

target.title: Expose plugin approval action metadata
target.body:
## Review context Concrete plugin: [Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit](https://github.com/Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit). The intent is to keep World ID / AgentKit out of OpenClaw core while letting external HITL plugins use the existing approval flow end-to-end. This PR's role: approval action metadata and no-route pending surface; it does not resolve verified approvals by itself. Bundle: - [#82431](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82431) exposes approval action metadata/UI rendering. - [#82434](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82434) adds verified approval resolution with plugin ownership checks. - [#82471](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82471) adds chat/status metadata and idempotency for durable approval updates. ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Agent participant Gateway participant Plugin as HITL plugin participant UI as TUI/Web UI participant Verifier as External verifier Agent->>Gateway: protected tool call Gateway->>Plugin: approval hook/request Gateway->>UI: approval card + plugin actions UI->>Plugin: user chooses verify once / trust session / deny Plugin->>Verifier: external proof flow Verifier-->>Plugin: verified result Plugin->>Gateway: resolve verified approval Gateway->>Agent: allow/deny blocked tool Plugin->>UI: status/update messages with idempotency ``` ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Approval action surface
#82431"] --> B["Verified resolver
#82434"] B --> C["Status/chat idempotency
#82471"] C --> D["Usable external HITL plugin flow"] ``` Trust boundary to review: should a plugin that already owns a pending approval be allowed to complete that specific approval after external verification, through a typed SDK/Gateway path with admin scope and ownership checks? Summary: - Add plugin approval action templates to the before_tool_call approval result and plugin.approval.request schema. - Expand templates with the server-generated approval id, store the resulting action commands, and carry them through reply payloads and approval view models. - Add keepPendingWithoutRoute so external HITL plugins can keep plugin approvals pending when no core approval route/client is active. - Keep native decision-button channel adapters compatible with command-only approval actions. - Update the generated Swift protocol model and plugin SDK API baseline for the intentional additive surface change. Verification: - `pnpm protocol:check` - `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check` - `pnpm tsgo:extensions` - `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke` - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts` - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts` - `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` ## Real behavior proof Behavior addressed: external plugins can attach approval-specific command actions to plugin approval requests and keep those requests pending while their own HITL command flow is responsible for resolution. Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, rebased onto current `origin/main` on May 16, 2026. Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran the protocol generator/check, plugin SDK API baseline check, extension typecheck, plugin SDK strict declaration smoke, targeted extension oxlint, focused approval/gateway/plugin Vitest coverage, the host hook contract test, and whitespace diff check. Evidence after fix: ```text $ pnpm protocol:check wrote dist/protocol.schema.json wrote apps/shared/OpenClawKit/Sources/OpenClawProtocol/GatewayModels.swift $ pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256 $ pnpm tsgo:extensions (exit 0) $ pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke (exit 0) $ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.ts extensions/telegram/src/approval-handler.runtime.ts Found 0 warnings and 0 errors. $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/plugin-approval.test.ts src/infra/approval-view-model.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/approval-renderers.test.ts extensions/discord/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/matrix/src/approval-handler.runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/bridge/approval/handler-runtime.test.ts extensions/qqbot/src/engine/approval/index.test.ts src/agents/pi-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts src/plugins/hooks.before-tool-call.test.ts Test Files 10 passed (10) Tests 122 passed (122) $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/contracts/host-hooks.contract.test.ts Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 50 passed (50) $ git diff --check origin/main...HEAD (no output) ``` Observed result after fix: the protocol and SDK baselines reflect the new additive action metadata, representative adapters preserve command actions, approval view/reply rendering carries those commands, and the before_tool_call host hook contract remains compatible. What was not tested: full release suite, live World ID callback flow, and the follow-up verified plugin-owned resolution API that is split into #82434. Refs #82336 ## External plugin integration proof Behavior addressed: the external ClawHub AgentKit plugin can consume the combined temporary OpenClaw host API branch that includes this PR's approval actions and no-route pending behavior. Real environment tested: GitHub Actions in `Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit` on May 16, 2026, checking out `Guardiola31337/openclaw@agentkit/external-plugin-host-apis` and linking it into the external plugin package. Exact steps or command run after this patch: the remote workflow checked out the temporary OpenClaw host branch, installed with frozen lockfiles, ran `pnpm build gatewayWatch`, ran `pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke`, linked the host checkout into the AgentKit plugin, then ran `pnpm test:hitl` and `pnpm pack:check`. Evidence after fix: ```text $ gh run watch 25972624778 --repo Guardiola31337/openclaw-agentkit --exit-status Ō£ō main CI ┬Ę 25972624778 Ō£ō build in 1m46s Ō£ō Run pnpm build gatewayWatch Ō£ō Run pnpm build:plugin-sdk:strict-smoke Ō£ō Run pnpm dev:link-openclaw .openclaw-source Ō£ō Run pnpm test:hitl Ō£ō Run pnpm pack:check ``` Observed result after fix: the external plugin builds against the split OpenClaw host APIs, the plugin SDK declarations expose the needed approval/action surface, the AgentKit HITL proof passes, and the package dry-run includes the expected ClawHub plugin artifact. What was not tested: production World ID credential verification. This remote proof exercises the integrated temporary host branch because the external plugin requires this PR together with #82434 and #82471 for the full HITL path.

target.title: fix(webchat): stabilize live transcript run state
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83528 and #82611; refs #83949.

- Mirror Codex app-server inbound prompts at turn start with the existing `${turnId}:prompt` identity so WebChat can see external-channel user messages before the turn finishes, while preserving `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence` for already-persisted inbound prompts.
- Keep hidden external-channel live runs private by sending chat/tool/agent updates only to exact selected-session message subscribers instead of broadcasting globally.
- Subscribe Control UI to the selected session message stream on connect/reconnect and session switch, adopt observed run ids for non-WebChat-originated runs including the default main alias/canonical key pair, dedupe accumulated stream snapshots around tool cards, and keep selected-session `session.message` reloads alias-aware.
- Emit stable `messageSeq` values for multi-message Codex mirror batches so selected-session subscribers receive per-message transcript positions instead of post-batch duplicates.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: WebChat/Control UI now has durable inbound prompt truth at Codex turn start, selected-session live updates for external-channel runs without unrelated-session leakage, passive run-id adoption and `session.message` handling across the default main alias/canonical key pair, reconnect-safe selected-session subscription state, stable mirrored transcript message sequences, suppression-safe prompt mirroring, and no duplicated accumulated stream text around tool cards.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on branch `meow/webchat-transcript-run-state-truth`, latest head `e19523ba6c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

commands.author: MichaelZelbel

target.title: fix(usage-cost): surface unpriced-model spend as missingCostEntries instead of a confident $0
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** `openclaw gateway usage-cost` reports `totalCost: 0` with `missingCostEntries: 0` for models that ship an all-zero `cost` block (e.g. codex `gpt-5.5`, whose Codex backend exposes no per-token price). Real pay-per-token usage is therefore reported as a confident, *complete* `$0`, and every budget / spike / daily-spend safeguard that keys off `totalCost` is silently blind to it.
- **Solution:** In `scanTranscriptFile`, when a model's resolved pricing is *unknown* ŌĆö no positive per-token rate and no tiered pricing ŌĆö and the turn actually burned tokens, drop the transport's fabricated `$0` and surface the turn as a missing-cost entry instead of recording a confident `$0`.
- **What changed:** `src/infra/session-cost-usage.ts` ŌĆö new `isModelPricingKnown()` helper and a sibling branch in `scanTranscriptFile` that mirrors the existing tiered-pricing override. `src/infra/session-cost-usage.test.ts` ŌĆö regression test.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** Models with positive or tiered pricing are unaffected; **operator-configured zero-cost models keep reporting a complete `$0`** (intentional free pricing is preserved); entries with zero token usage are not flagged; a transport-recorded *positive* cost is preserved; recorded costs for priced models are untouched. No change to model-catalog generation or the gateway pricing cache.

## Motivation

A freshly-onboarded, idle host on `2026.5.20` (default `openai/gpt-5.5`, `api_key` auth) accrued ~1.78M tokens/day, yet `usage-cost` reported `$0` with `missingCostEntries: 0`, so budget/spike monitoring never fired. Surfacing unknown-priced usage as *missing* lets downstream tooling tell "genuinely free" apart from "price unknown" instead of trusting a false `$0`. Tracks #85858.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #85858
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `usage-cost` reports a confident `$0` (`missingCostEntries: 0`) for unpriced `gpt-5.5` usage, hiding real spend from budget/spike monitoring.
- Real environment tested: self-hosted OpenClaw `2026.5.20 (e510042)` on Linux; default model `openai/gpt-5.5`; `api_key` auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: applied the equivalent change to the installed build, restarted the gateway, then ran `openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json --expect-final` over the same already-logged usage.
- Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output of the `openclaw gateway usage-cost` command ŌĆö before vs. after the patch, same logs:

```
BEFORE (shipped build) -- openclaw gateway usage-cost --days 2 --json
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 0 }

AFTER (patched build) -- same command, same already-logged usage
  totals: { totalTokens: 1780235, totalCost: 0, missingCostEntries: 32 }
```

- Observed result after fix: the same 1,780,235 tokens of otherwise-invisible unpriced usage are now surfaced as 32 missing-cost entries; `totalCost` stays `0` (no fabricated price is invented).
- What was not tested: models configured with *intentional* zero pricing (these would also be surfaced as missing ŌĆö see scope note below); the macOS/Control-UI cost views.
- Before evidence: included in the terminal capture above.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- **Root cause:** codex models ship `cost: {input:0, output:0, cacheRead:0, cacheWrite:0}` in the generated `models.json` because the Codex backend exposes no per-token price. `resolveModelCostConfig` returns that *defined* zero, `estimateUsageCost` computes a confident `0`, and the transport records `cost.total: 0` per turn. Cost aggregation (`applyCostTotal` / `applyCostBreakdown`) only treats `undefined` ŌĆö not `0` ŌĆö as missing, so "price unknown" is indistinguishable from "genuinely free."

## Note on the free-vs-unknown distinction

This fix is **source-aware** so it does not break intentionally-free pricing. A turn's all-zero cost is treated as *unknown* (counted toward `missingCostEntries`) only when **all** of these hold: the resolved pricing has no positive/tiered rate, the operator did **not** explicitly configure the model's price under `models.providers` (so the zero is a generated-catalog default, e.g. codex/gpt-5.x), and there is no trustworthy recorded cost (recorded cost is `0`/absent), and the turn actually burned tokens. A model the operator **deliberately** priced at `0`, or any turn carrying a real positive recorded cost, keeps reporting a complete `$0`. (Helper `resolveConfiguredModelCost()` reads config-only pricing to make this distinction; both paths have regression tests.)

Residual nuance for maintainers: this preserves *operator-configured* zero prices, but a non-codex provider whose models are emitted into the generated catalog with literal zeros would still be treated as unknown. Fully distinguishing that case wants an explicit pricing contract (e.g. `cost: null`/"unknown" vs `cost: 0`/"free") in the catalog schema ŌĆö happy to follow whichever contract you prefer.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(gateway): add provider auth prewarm operator controls
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: provider auth prewarm can still be expensive during gateway startup or recovery, and affected operators do not have a supported way to disable or delay it.
- Solution: expose a gateway-level provider auth prewarm policy with config and env controls.
- What changed: added `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled`, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.delayMs`, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM`; startup, reload, logout-triggered rewarm, delayed startup prewarm, and auth-profile-failure rewarm paths now honor the resolved policy.
- Docs: documented the new config keys and env override in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and updated the generated config baseline hash.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not claim to fix the underlying event-loop starvation in `warmCurrentProviderAuthState`, Discord READY timing, or OpenAI/Codex route fallback behavior.

## Motivation

This PR follows up on the Discord/operator recovery report in #85997. It also relates to the narrower provider-auth startup/prewarm reproduction in #85999.

This proposes an operator safety valve while maintainers evaluate the deeper scheduling/caching fix for provider auth prewarm. It is intentionally not marked as closing either issue because the user-facing reports cover broader startup, channel readiness, and auth-provider routing behavior.

AI-assisted: drafted and implemented with Codex; human review and maintainer product feedback are requested before merge.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Follow-up to #85997.
- Related to #85999.
- This PR does not close either issue; it proposes a scoped mitigation for provider auth prewarm operator control.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: affected deployments can now disable provider auth prewarm or increase its startup delay without patching OpenClaw internals.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu Linux server. Startup proof was captured on this branch before the final sidecar-policy repair; the branch is now rebased on `origin/main` at `483d7be6c4` with current head `3f3fb0361c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Started a real gateway process from this checkout with an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
  - Used `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1` to keep the run hermetic while still exercising the gateway startup/post-ready path.
  - Ran three redacted startup cases: default config, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`, and config-enabled plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
- Evidence after fix (redacted terminal/log proof):

Default config still schedules and runs provider auth prewarm:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=1.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] provider auth state pre-warmed in 75ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] default-enabled elapsedMs=16851 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Config disable skips the provider auth prewarm sidecar:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.5ms total=5670.5ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] config-disabled elapsedMs=10409 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Env override skips prewarm even when config enables it:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.3ms total=5482.6ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] env-disabled elapsedMs=10152 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

- Targeted verification:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts` -> 3 files passed, 88 tests passed after final rebase.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 111 tests passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm config:docs:check` -> passed after final rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` -> passed after final rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
- Observed result after fix: default behavior remains enabled; config/env disable paths skip startup prewarm; delayed startup prewarm and auth-profile-failure rewarm re-check the current policy before running; reload and logout rewarm paths skip when the same policy disables provider auth prewarm.
- What was not tested: live Discord restart timing, real provider CLI starvation on a constrained VM, and OpenAI Codex OAuth fallback behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #85999 includes startup prewarm timing logs; #85997 includes redacted Discord/operator-visible errors.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current main already defers provider auth prewarm, but the exposed prewarm controls are internal test/runtime options rather than an operator-facing config surface.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no schema-covered config/env option to disable or delay provider auth prewarm on deployments where the prewarm path harms startup or channel recovery.
- Contributing context (if known): #85272 narrowed startup ordering by deferring prewarm; #85999 reports that provider auth prewarm can still monopolize startup on a constrained host.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`, `src/config/config-misc.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: disabled prewarm policy skips startup/reload/logout rewarm paths, env override disables config-enabled prewarm, configured delay is honored, and a long-lived provider-auth sidecar re-checks current config before delayed startup or auth-profile-failure rewarm.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the change is policy resolution and call-site gating; these tests cover each touched call path without requiring live providers.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): startup prewarm ordering coverage existed, but not operator-facing config/env policy.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds optional gateway config and env controls:

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "providerAuthPrewarm": {
      "enabled": false,
      "delayMs": 5000
    }
  }
}
```

`OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1` disables provider auth prewarm regardless of config.

Defaults remain unchanged: provider auth prewarm stays enabled with the existing startup delay.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [provider auth prewarm always scheduled]

After:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [resolve config/env policy] -> [schedule or skip provider auth prewarm]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux server
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: provider behavior mocked in tests; real gateway startup proof used isolated temp config/state
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway startup/reload/logout paths; no live Discord run
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic gateway config with `auth.mode: none`, `controlUi.enabled: false`, temporary port, and no real channel/provider credentials

### Steps

1. Start gateway with default synthetic config and startup trace enabled.
2. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`.
3. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: true` plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
4. Run targeted gateway/config tests, config docs check, and core typecheck.

### Expected

- Default policy schedules and runs provider auth prewarm.
- Disabled policy skips provider auth prewarm.
- Env override wins over config-enabled prewarm.
- Configured delay controls startup prewarm delay.
- Long-lived sidecar rewarm paths respect the current config after hot reload.

### Actual

- Matches expected in redacted real gateway startup logs, targeted tests, local typechecks, and docs check.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real gateway startup proof for default/config-disabled/env-disabled behavior; targeted gateway/config tests; docs baseline check; `tsgo:core`; `tsgo:core:test`; `tsgo:extensions:test`.
- Edge cases checked: env override disabling config-enabled prewarm; invalid schema values rejected; reload/logout rewarm paths honor the same policy; long-lived sidecar skips delayed startup and auth-profile-failure rewarm after current config disables prewarm.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord startup recovery, real constrained-host prewarm timing, and provider OAuth fallback behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration required; operators may opt into the new config/env controls.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: disabling prewarm may make model/provider auth status colder until first use.
  - Mitigation: default behavior remains enabled; the disable path is explicit opt-in for affected operators.
- Risk: this may be considered a product/config surface decision rather than the preferred root fix.
  - Mitigation: scoped as a mitigation, and does not close the linked issues.

target.title: feat(plugins): add optional api.resetSession()
target.body:
## Summary

`main` already has gateway-side session reset support through `performGatewaySessionReset()`.

This PR adds optional plugin-side `api.resetSession(key, reason?)` support on top of the current mainline architecture.

- Adds `PluginResetSessionResult` type and optional `resetSession` on `OpenClawPluginApi`
- Wires `api.resetSession(...)` in the plugin registry only in full registration mode and only when the gateway exposes `sessions.reset`
- Reuses gateway-owned canonicalization and reset resolution (no plugin-side duplicate logic)
- Unifies config snapshot usage in `performGatewaySessionReset` to eliminate config-drift between target resolution and entry lookup
- Adds per-canonical-key in-flight guard in the reset service
- Exports `PluginResetSessionResult` from `openclaw/plugin-sdk/core`

## Scope

This PR:
- adds `PluginResetSessionResult` to `src/plugins/types.ts`
- adds optional `resetSession` to `OpenClawPluginApi`
- adds builder support in `src/plugins/api-builder.ts`
- wires `api.resetSession(...)` in `src/plugins/registry.ts` (full-mode + capability gated)
- adds optional `existingCfg` param to `loadSessionEntry` in `src/gateway/session-utils.ts`
- adds in-flight guard and unified resolution in `src/gateway/session-reset-service.ts`
- exports the result type from `src/plugin-sdk/core.ts`
- adds focused plugin contract tests (17 tests)
- refreshes generated plugin SDK API baselines

This PR does not:
- change gateway reset RPC behavior
- broaden session architecture scope beyond the plugin API surface
- revive any stale-branch refactor baggage from the closed #43375

## Behavior

`api.resetSession(key, reason?)`:
- accepts `"new"` or `"reset"` reason values (defaults to `"new"`)
- trims and validates the key
- delegates to gateway-owned `performGatewaySessionReset`
- resolves to a structured plugin result instead of rejecting for operational failures
- absent in setup-only, setup-runtime, and cli-metadata registration modes
- absent when gateway does not expose `sessions.reset`

Success: `{ ok: true, key, sessionId }`
Failure: `{ ok: false, key, code, message }`

## Local Verification

```
pnpm test -- src/plugins/reset-session.test.ts     # 17/17 pass
pnpm test -- src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts      # 77/77 pass
pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check                           # OK
pnpm tsgo                                           # no new errors
```

Replaces #43375 (closed, stale branch).

target.title: fix(webchat): stabilize live transcript run state
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83528 and #82611; refs #83949.

- Mirror Codex app-server inbound prompts at turn start with the existing `${turnId}:prompt` identity so WebChat can see external-channel user messages before the turn finishes, while preserving `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence` for already-persisted inbound prompts.
- Keep hidden external-channel live runs private by sending chat/tool/agent updates only to exact selected-session message subscribers instead of broadcasting globally.
- Subscribe Control UI to the selected session message stream on connect/reconnect and session switch, adopt observed run ids for non-WebChat-originated runs including the default main alias/canonical key pair, dedupe accumulated stream snapshots around tool cards, and keep selected-session `session.message` reloads alias-aware.
- Emit stable `messageSeq` values for multi-message Codex mirror batches so selected-session subscribers receive per-message transcript positions instead of post-batch duplicates.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: WebChat/Control UI now has durable inbound prompt truth at Codex turn start, selected-session live updates for external-channel runs without unrelated-session leakage, passive run-id adoption and `session.message` handling across the default main alias/canonical key pair, reconnect-safe selected-session subscription state, stable mirrored transcript message sequences, suppression-safe prompt mirroring, and no duplicated accumulated stream text around tool cards.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on branch `meow/webchat-transcript-run-state-truth`, latest head `e19523ba6c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- -t "does not mirror the Codex prompt early when user message persistence is suppressed"`

  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node --import tsx scripts/check-no-extension-test-core-imports.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- Evidence after fix:
  - Suppression regression: failed before the guard because the forbidden prompt appeared; passed after the guard.
  - Codex app-server tests: 2 files passed, 233 tests passed.
  - Gateway/session event tests: 4 files passed, 111 tests passed.
  - Control UI tests: 7 files passed, 238 tests passed.
  - Rebased-base test-type command passed.
  - Boundary check passed: `check-no-extension-test-core-imports` checked 1915 extension files and 1 plugin helper.
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` passed on latest head.
  - The regressions prove message-bearing prompt transcript updates with message ids and stable sequences, suppressed prompt persistence does not early-mirror a duplicate user row, multi-message mirror batch `messageSeq` `[1, 2]`, selected-session sequence delivery, alias/canonical subscription de-dupe, stale subscription completion cleanup, main-alias `session.message` reloads, and passive main-alias run-id adoption.
- Observed result after fix: The early Codex prompt mirror writes one user transcript row and emits a message-bearing selected-session update before assistant completion only when prompt persistence is not suppressed; final mirrored batches emit correct per-message sequence metadata; hidden external-channel chat events route only to exact selected-session subscribers; selected Control UI sessions subscribe/re-subscribe without alias churn or stale async overwrite; canonical `agent:main:main` transcript events reload selected `main`; passive chat state adopts observed run ids; and accumulated stream snapshots render only their new suffix after tool cards.
- What was not tested: Live Feishu or Telegram external-channel browser proof was not run in this pass. Remote `pnpm check:changed` through Crabbox was attempted before this follow-up but blocked: Blacksmith Testbox is not configured for `.github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`, and default AWS Crabbox could not refresh credentials because no EC2 IMDS role/credentials were available on this machine.

target.title: Doctor: structure shell completion repair
target.body:
## Summary

- Converts shell completion cache/profile repair into a structured core doctor health check.
- Keeps shell completion positional: broad structured repairs skip it, then the existing shell-completion contribution runs the structured check at its original point in doctor order.
- Preserves the legacy optional install prompt path only when the structured check reports no finding, so stale cache/profile repairs are not run twice.
- Keeps optional missing shell completion quiet for lint and non-interactive repair, and makes no plugin, gateway, SDK, or public health contract changes.

## Verification

- pnpm install
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- git diff --check
- node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-completion.ts src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --noEmit --incremental false --pretty false
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-completion.test.ts src/flows/doctor-health-contributions.test.ts src/flows/doctor-core-checks.test.ts src/flows/doctor-repair-flow.test.ts --reporter=dot: 4 files / 47 tests passed
- codex review --base origin/main: no discrete correctness issues

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Shell completion doctor repair now emits structured findings/repair results for slow dynamic completion and installed profiles with missing completion caches, without making optional missing completion a lint failure.
Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu 24.04, source checkout CLI, disposable HOME with a fake `.zshrc` and isolated OpenClaw state dir.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a disposable HOME, wrote `.zshrc` with `source <(openclaw completion zsh)`, pre-created `.openclaw/completions/openclaw.zsh`, then ran `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion --json`, `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes --no-workspace-suggestions`, and `openclaw doctor --lint --only core/doctor/shell-completion --json` again through the PR source checkout.
Evidence after fix: Before fix, lint returned `ok:false` with one `core/doctor/shell-completion` warning: `Your zsh profile uses slow dynamic completion (source <(...)).` During fix, doctor printed `Shell completion upgraded. Restart your shell or run: source ~/.zshrc` and completed. After fix, lint returned `ok:true` with `findings:[]`. The profile was rewritten to source `<TMP_HOME>/.openclaw/completions/openclaw.zsh`.
Observed result after fix: The migrated structured repair path upgrades a slow dynamic zsh completion profile to the cached completion source line, keeps the shell-completion check positional, and the same `core/doctor/shell-completion` lint selector is clean after repair.
What was not tested: Full repo check in this rerun; live mutation of a real user shell profile. The disposable source-checkout proof avoided the missing-cache cache-generation branch because this worktree currently has an unrelated source launcher rebuild blocker in bundled diagnostics plugin packaging; focused tests cover missing-cache regeneration.

target.title: fix(gateway): dedupe probe warnings by gateway identity
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw gateway probe` warned about multiple reachable gateways when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes reached the same gateway through different transports.
- Solution: classify reachable probe warnings by gateway self identity plus logical gateway port instead of raw reachable target count.
- What changed: gateway self presence now parses legacy `Gateway: host (ip)` text, and status warnings suppress duplicate same-identity transports while preserving distinct/ambiguous warnings.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): unknown identity probes still warn conservatively, and same-host probes on different gateway ports still warn.

## Motivation

- Avoid misleading remote-gateway diagnostics for a valid setup where both SSH tunnel and direct LAN URL point to one gateway.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #69135
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior addressed: `gateway probe` no longer emits `multiple_gateways` for two reachable transports that identify the same gateway and same logical gateway port.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu-24.04 source checkout using a Linux network namespace as the remote host, with a temporary sshd, real `/usr/bin/ssh` local port forward, direct remote WebSocket URL, and paired `operator.read` device auth.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts --reporter=dot`; then `node --import tsx src/entry.ts gateway probe --ssh root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port> --ssh-identity <temp-key> --timeout 20000 --json` against a namespace-hosted gateway with `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`.
- Evidence after fix: focused tests passed with 2 files and 10 tests. The live CLI proof returned `ok: true`, `degraded: false`, `warnings: []`, `primaryTargetId: "sshTunnel"`, and two reachable targets: `sshTunnel` at `ws://127.0.0.1:<gateway-port>` and `configRemote` at `ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, both reporting the same gateway self identity and `operator.read` scope.
- Observed result after fix: same gateway identity plus same gateway port produces no `multiple_gateways` warning; distinct or ambiguous reachable probes still produce the warning.
- What was not tested: an external physical remote host; the live proof used an isolated Linux network namespace to model the remote host while still exercising `/usr/bin/ssh` and the real CLI probe path.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: `buildGatewayStatusWarnings` treated `reachable.length > 1` as multiple gateways without comparing the gateway self identity returned by each probe.
- Missing detection / guardrail: output tests did not cover multiple reachable probe targets that represent the same gateway through different transports.
- Contributing context (if known): SSH tunnel probing prepends a local tunnel URL while keeping the configured remote URL, so two reachable targets can be one logical gateway.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/commands/gateway-status/output.test.ts`, `src/commands/gateway-presence.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: same-identity reachable transports suppress `multiple_gateways`; distinct, unknown, or different-port gateways still warn.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in warning construction after probe results are already collected.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw gateway probe` no longer shows the multiple-gateways warning when SSH tunnel and configured remote probes identify the same gateway.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [reachable count > 1] -> multiple_gateways

After:
[ssh tunnel target reachable] + [configured remote reachable] -> [same self identity + gateway port] -> no warning
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: WSL Ubuntu-24.04
- Runtime/container: Node 22 repo checkout
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): Gateway status/probe output
- Relevant config (redacted): `gateway.mode=remote`, `gateway.remote.url=ws://<remote-ip>:<gateway-port>`, `gateway.remote.sshTarget=root@<remote-ip>:<ssh-port>`, paired device auth with `operator.read`; remote gateway bound on LAN in a Linux netns.

### Steps

1. Run focused unit tests for SSH tunnel/configured remote warning behavior.
2. Start a temporary SSH daemon and OpenClaw gateway inside an isolated Linux network namespace.
3. Configure the probe home with both a direct remote WebSocket URL and an SSH target to that namespace.
4. Run `openclaw gateway probe --ssh ... --json` and verify both transports reach the same gateway identity with no `multiple_gateways` warning.

### Expected

- Same identity and same logical port: no `multiple_gateways`.
- Distinct or ambiguous gateway identities: keep `multiple_gateways`.

### Actual

- Matches expected after this patch.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: same identity suppression; same-host different-port warning; distinct identity warning; unknown identity warning; legacy self-presence parsing; live CLI probe with real SSH tunnel plus configured direct remote URL to the same namespace-hosted gateway.
- Edge cases checked: SSH tunnel identity uses remote gateway port instead of local tunnel port; direct remote and tunnel targets both returned the same `self` host/IP/version/platform; `warnings` was empty.
- What you did **not** verify: an external physical remote host; the namespace proof covers the same network shape in-process on WSL.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: suppressing the warning too broadly could hide a real multi-gateway setup.
  - Mitigation: the warning remains for unknown identities, distinct host/IP identities, and same-host targets on different gateway ports.

target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and the inbound peer for the current automatic-delivery turn, including `identityLinks` aliases.
- Threads inbound peer context through automatic replies, routed replies, tool context, plugin/gateway message-action context, and durable outbound delivery.
- Keeps send-policy-only predicates out of shared allow/deny scopes.
- Preserves explicit `message.send` compatibility by keeping explicit gateway/plugin/direct sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.
- Reports blocked automatic durable sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs/help labels for the new predicate.

Fixes #85175.

target.title: feat(ui): timestamp unnamed dashboard sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: new Control UI dashboard sessions without a label/displayName fall back to raw session keys, making multiple new chats hard to distinguish.
- Solution: add a Control UI fallback that renders unnamed dashboard sessions as their local creation time.
- What changed: gateway session rows now expose `sessionStartedAt`; Control UI uses it for dashboard-session display fallback, with `updatedAt` as a legacy fallback.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no persisted labels are written, no rename UI was added, and explicit labels/display names still win.

AI-assisted: yes. I used a local coding agent and ran the verification below myself.

## Motivation

- Closes #86040.
- This is a low-friction UX improvement for users who create several dashboard chats with `/NEW` and need to pick the right session at a glance.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #86040
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: unnamed Control UI dashboard sessions now display a local timestamp fallback instead of the raw `agent:<id>:dashboard:<uuid>` key, while named sessions still display their explicit label.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.5 (25F71), local OpenClaw checkout at this PR branch, Node v23.11.0, pnpm 11.2.2.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `pnpm --dir ui exec tsx -e "import { resolveSessionDisplayName } from './src/ui/session-display.ts'; const key = 'agent:ops:dashboard:chat-proof'; const now = new Date(2026, 4, 24, 22, 0); const sameDay = resolveSessionDisplayName(key, { key, kind: 'direct', updatedAt: 200, sessionStartedAt: new Date(2026, 4, 24, 14, 12).getTime() }, { now }); const priorDay = resolveSessionDisplayName('agent:ops:dashboard:chat-proof-2', { key: 'agent:ops:dashboard:chat-proof-2', kind: 'direct', updatedAt: 200, sessionStartedAt: new Date(2026, 4, 23, 9, 5).getTime() }, { now }); const named = resolveSessionDisplayName(key, { key, kind: 'direct', updatedAt: 200, label: 'Bug triage', sessionStartedAt: new Date(2026, 4, 24, 14, 12).getTime() }, { now }); console.log(JSON.stringify({ sameDay, priorDay, named }, null, 2));"`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```json
{
  "sameDay": "14:12",
  "priorDay": "05-23 09:05",
  "named": "Bug triage"
}
```

- Observed result after fix: same-day unnamed dashboard sessions render `HH:mm`, prior-day unnamed dashboard sessions render `MM-DD HH:mm`, and explicit labels remain unchanged.
- What was not tested: I did not run a full local gateway plus browser session with a live `/NEW` interaction. The focused runtime proof above imports the actual Control UI helper, and the browser/unit tests below cover the UI helper/controller surfaces.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new RED test initially failed with `expected 'agent:ops:dashboard:chat-1' to be '14:12'` before the implementation.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: N/A, this is a feature request.
- Missing detection / guardrail: unnamed dashboard-session display fallback was not covered.
- Contributing context (if known): dashboard session rows already had durable `sessionStartedAt`, but the projected gateway row did not expose it to the UI.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts` and `src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: unnamed dashboard sessions use `sessionStartedAt` for timestamp fallback; explicit labels still win; gateway rows include `sessionStartedAt`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the behavior is pure display resolution plus one projected row field.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): none.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

target.title: fix(doctor): recover sibling moltbot config
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #54200 by adding a narrow recovery path for the upgrade layout where `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` is missing or skeletal while the user's real legacy config still exists at `~/.openclaw/moltbot.json`.

- `doctor` preflight copies sibling `moltbot.json` into canonical `openclaw.json` when the canonical file is missing.
- If canonical `openclaw.json` exists but is skeletal, read-only doctor now only warns; `openclaw doctor --fix` backs it up and restores from sibling `moltbot.json`.
- The replacement path copies through a temp file, restores the original canonical config on failure, and chmods the recovered canonical config to `0600`.
- Gateway startup adds a targeted hint when `gateway.mode` is missing and the sibling legacy config exists.

This does **not** add `moltbot.json` back to the normal config resolver or public config candidate list.

## Verification

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts --reporter=dot` (2 files, 35 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/paths.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-flow.test.ts src/commands/doctor-config-preflight.test.ts src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts --reporter=dot` (4 files, 92 tests passed)
- `git diff --check refs/remotes/origin/main...HEAD`

`pnpm check:changed` reaches an unrelated current-main core-test typecheck failure in `scripts/control-ui-i18n.ts` for missing declarations on `./npm-runner.mjs`, `./pnpm-runner.mjs`, and `./windows-cmd-helpers.mjs`. The core production typecheck lane passed before that failure.

## Real behavior proof

Real temp home: `/tmp/openclaw-54200-proof-2ODsvV` with:

- skeletal canonical config: `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` containing only `update.channel`
- sibling legacy config: `~/.openclaw/moltbot.json` containing `gateway.mode=local`, `gateway.port=19091`, and `agents.defaults.model`
- legacy file mode before repair: `0644`

Commands run from this branch:

```bash
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-54200-proof-2ODsvV OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts doctor --no-workspace-suggestions --non-interactive
OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-54200-proof-2ODsvV OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 OPENCLAW_TEST_FAST=1 node --import tsx src/entry.ts doctor --no-workspace-suggestions --non-interactive --fix
```

Observed after read-only doctor:

- Output included: `Found legacy sibling config at /tmp/openclaw-54200-proof-2ODsvV/.openclaw/moltbot.json; run openclaw doctor --fix to recover it into /tmp/openclaw-54200-proof-2ODsvV/.openclaw/openclaw.json.`
- `openclaw.json` remained the original skeletal file.
- No `openclaw.json.pre-moltbot-migration.*` backup was created by read-only doctor.

Observed after `doctor --fix`:

- Output included `Backed up skeletal config` and `Migrated legacy config` for `moltbot.json -> openclaw.json`.
- `openclaw.json` contained recovered `gateway.mode: "local"`, `gateway.port: 19091`, and `agents.defaults.model: "openai/gpt-5.5"`.
- Backup existed: `openclaw.json.pre-moltbot-migration.2026-05-24T13-43-20-209Z`.
- Final canonical config permissions: `mode=600 path=/tmp/openclaw-54200-proof-2ODsvV/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.

target.title: fix(secrets): allow hash in exec SecretRef ids
target.body:
## Summary
- allow `#` in exec SecretRef ids while preserving traversal-segment rejection
- add a shared valid test vector for the documented AWS Secrets Manager `secret#json_key` convention
- keep config, plan, gateway protocol, and plugin SDK validation in parity
- sync the public configuration reference with the broadened exec SecretRef id pattern

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** exec SecretRef ids that include an AWS-style JSON-key selector such as `gravity/shared/slack-gateway/credentials#bot_token` should pass OpenClaw config validation and reach the exec provider path instead of being rejected by the SecretRef id contract.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw CLI on macOS from branch `fix/secretref-exec-hash-ids`, with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` pointed at isolated `/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof` files so no user gateway state was touched.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/80712-openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/state-80712 pnpm openclaw config validate --json`
- **Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from the real CLI run:

  ```text
  $ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/80712-openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/state-80712 pnpm openclaw config validate --json
  $ node scripts/run-node.mjs config validate --json
  {"valid":true,"path":"/private/tmp/openclaw-pr-proof/80712-openclaw.json"}
  ```

- **Observed result after fix:** The config containing `models.providers.openai.apiKey.id = "gravity/shared/slack-gateway/credentials#bot_token"` returned `valid: true`, confirming the real OpenClaw config validation path now accepts `#json_key` exec SecretRef ids.
- **Not tested:** Did not call a live AWS Secrets Manager resolver; this proof verifies the OpenClaw validation/gateway config path accepts the documented exec SecretRef id so the configured provider can resolve it at runtime.

## Validation
- `pnpm test src/secrets/ref-contract.test.ts src/secrets/exec-secret-ref-id-parity.test.ts src/config/config.secrets-schema.test.ts src/gateway/protocol/primitives.secretref.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/secret-input.test.ts src/secrets/plan.test.ts src/secrets/resolve.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md src/secrets/ref-contract.ts src/secrets/ref-contract.test.ts src/secrets/exec-secret-ref-id-parity.test.ts src/test-utils/secret-ref-test-vectors.ts`
- `git diff --check`

Fixes #80712

target.title: Control UI: add toggle to expand tool call output by default in webchat (fixes #54478)
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** In the webchat (Control UI), tool call output is always collapsed by default. Users must manually click to expand each output, which is inconvenient when debugging or tracking agent workflows.
- **Why it matters:** The TUI already supports this via `Ctrl+O` / `/verbose on`, but the webchat has no equivalent toggle.
- **What changed:** Added a new `chatExpandToolCalls` setting with a toggle button in the chat controls toolbar (both desktop and mobile). When enabled, all `<details>` elements wrapping tool calls and tool outputs render with the `open` attribute, expanding them by default.
- **What did NOT change:** Backend, session data, tool call rendering logic, or existing `showToolCalls` (show/hide) behavior. The expand toggle is disabled when tool calls are hidden.

## Change Type

- [x] Feature

## Scope

- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #54478

## Root Cause / Regression History

N/A ŌĆö new feature.

## Regression Test Plan

N/A ŌĆö new feature.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New toggle button (unfold icon) in chat controls toolbar, next to the existing tool calls toggle.
- When active, all tool call sections and tool output sections are expanded by default.
- Setting is persisted in localStorage.
- Toggle is disabled when tool calls are hidden (`chatShowToolCalls: false`).

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

All 35 chat tests + 1 gateway session test pass. Settings persistence tested via existing storage test patterns.

## Human Verification

- Verified: `chatExpandToolCalls` defaults to `false`, toggles correctly, persists across reloads.
- Edge cases: disabled state when `showToolCalls=false`, onboarding mode behavior.
- Not verified: live gateway with real tool call messages (no access to running instance).

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes (new setting defaults to `false`, matching current behavior)
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to disable/revert: Revert this commit; tool calls will return to always-collapsed default.
- Known bad symptoms: If `chatExpandToolCalls` is missing from persisted settings, it gracefully defaults to `false`.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Large conversations with many tool calls may render slower when all expanded.
  - Mitigation: Default is `false` (collapsed); user opts in explicitly.

## Files Changed (11)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `ui/src/ui/storage.ts` | Added `chatExpandToolCalls` to `UiSettings`, defaults, parsing, persistence |
| `ui/src/ui/chat/grouped-render.ts` | Added `expandToolCalls` opt, `?open=` on `<details>` elements |
| `ui/src/ui/views/chat.ts` | Added `expandToolCalls` to `ChatProps`, passed to `renderMessageGroup` |
| `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` | Derived `expandToolCalls` from settings, passed to chat view |
| `ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.ts` | Added expand toggle button (desktop + mobile) with unfold icon |
| `ui/src/i18n/locales/en.ts` | Added `expandToolCallsToggle` i18n string |
| 5 test files | Added `chatExpandToolCalls: false` / `expandToolCalls: false` to test fixtures |
