target.title: fix/agents-primary-model-selection-and-catalog-loading-41344
target.body: ## Summary - Fixes incorrect Primary Model selection when switching between agents in Agents > Overview. - Fixes partial / blank Primary Model dropdowns after refresh or first load by ensuring the model catalog is available on Agents Overview paths. - Adds targeted tests for both selection stability and catalog hydration behavior. - Keeps the fix scoped to the Agents Overview model-selection path. ## Change Type (select all) - [x] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [x] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [ ] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [ ] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [x] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Related #41344 - Related #46275 - [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression ## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, explain why this happened, not just what changed. Otherwise write `N/A`. If the cause is unclear, write `Unknown`. - Root cause: 1. The agent details subtree could reuse stale DOM/select state when switching agents, which caused the Primary Model select to drift toward the previously viewed agent's state. 2. Agents Overview depended on a shared model catalog that was not guaranteed to be loaded on refresh / first-load / overview-only paths, so the select could render before the catalog was ready and degrade into a partial or blank-looking list. - Missing detection / guardrail: - Existing coverage covered some selected-value behavior, but it did not cover model-catalog hydration timing or the refresh/overview path where the catalog starts empty and arrives later. - Prior context (`git blame`, prior PR, issue, or refactor if known): - Prior work around #41344 / #46275 addressed the agent-switching selection issue. Revalidating that fix on latest `upstream/main` exposed a second, related lifecycle issue in Agents Overview model-catalog loading. - Why this regressed now: - The Overview UI depends on model-catalog data, but not all entry paths guaranteed that the catalog was loaded before rendering. - If unknown, what was ruled out: - Ruled out a pure selected-state-only explanation. The partial/blank dropdown after refresh was caused by catalog availability/lifecycle, not just DOM selection drift. ## Regression Test Plan (if applicable) For bug fixes or regressions, name the smallest reliable test coverage that should have caught this. Otherwise write `N/A`. - Coverage level that should have caught this: - [x] Unit test - [x] Seam / integration test - [ ] End-to-end test - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient - Target test or file: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.browser.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.test.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.test.ts` - Scenario the test should lock in: - Switching between agents keeps the correct explicit Primary Model selected. - The inherit-default option text does not go blank. - When the model catalog is initially empty, Agents Overview shows a loading state instead of a misleading partial list. - When the catalog hydrates later, the full list appears without losing the selected value. - Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: - The bug spans both UI render behavior and shared model-catalog lifecycle, so a combination of focused controller/unit tests plus browser-level select behavior tests is the smallest reliable coverage. - Existing test that already covers this (if any): - Existing browser coverage covered some selection behavior, but not the catalog hydration path. - If no new test is added, why not: - N/A ## User-visible / Behavior Changes List user-visible changes (including defaults/config). If none, write `None`. - Switching between agents no longer causes the Primary Model select to show the wrong agent's value. - On refresh / first entry to Agents Overview, the Primary Model select no longer degrades into a misleading partial list. - While the model catalog is still loading, the UI now shows a loading state instead of pretending only a small subset of options exists. - Once the catalog loads, the full option list appears without losing the current selection. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No - New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No - Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: - N/A ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: Windows 10 - Runtime/container: local dev environment - Model/provider: local UI validation only - Integration/channel (if any): N/A - Relevant config (redacted): standard Agents UI configuration with default and non-default agents ### Steps 1. Open Agents > Overview > Model Selection > Primary Model. 2. Switch between a default agent and a non-default agent. 3. Refresh the page and reopen the Primary Model select immediately. 4. Optionally wait for model-catalog hydration or switch away/back to Overview. ### Expected - The select reflects the correct explicit value for the current agent. - The inherit-default option text is stable. - Refresh / first-load does not show a misleading partial catalog. - Once the catalog is available, the full model list appears without losing the selected value. ### Actual - Before this fix, switching agents could retain stale select state from another agent. - Before this fix, refresh / first-load could show only fallback options (for example inherit/current) or appear blank until the catalog became available later. ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [x] Failing test/log before + passing after - [ ] Trace/log snippets - [x] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ### UI Evidence #### Before After refresh / first load, the Primary Model dropdown could show only fallback entries or appear blank. #### After After the fix, the Primary Model dropdown restores the full option list correctly after refresh / first load, without losing the selected value. ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: - Switched repeatedly between default and non-default agents and confirmed Primary Model stays tied to the current agent. - Refreshed the page and reopened Agents Overview to verify the select no longer falls into a misleading partial/blank state. - Confirmed catalog hydration restores the full list without losing the selected value. - Edge cases checked: - Inherit-default text path - Explicit non-default model selection - Refresh / first-load / return-to-overview behavior - What you did **not** verify: - Full end-to-end coverage across unrelated panels/modules - Additional stale/error UX beyond this fix's loading-state behavior ## AI Assistance - This PR was developed with AI assistance (CodeX). - I reviewed the resulting changes, reproduced the bug locally, and verified the final behavior manually. - Testing level: fully tested locally with targeted automated tests and manual UI verification. ## Review Conversations - [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes - Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No - Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No - If yes, exact upgrade steps: - N/A ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: - Revert this PR's two commits. - Files/config to restore: - `ui/src/ui/views/agents.ts` - `ui/src/ui/views/agents-panels-overview.ts` - `ui/src/ui/controllers/models.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-render.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-settings.ts` - `ui/src/ui/app-chat.ts` - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: - Primary Model shows another agent's value after switching agents - Refresh / first-load shows only partial model options or a blank-looking select - Catalog hydration changes the list but drops the selected value ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: - Multiple UI entry points now ensure model-catalog availability, which could increase repeated refresh attempts if not properly deduped. - Mitigation: - The loading/ensure logic is centralized in the shared model controller and covered by targeted tests. - Risk: - Keyed subtree remounting can discard unsaved local UI state when switching agents. - Mitigation: - That tradeoff is intentional here because correctness of agent-specific model selection is more important than preserving stale subtree state across agent switches.

target.title: fix(codex): ensure codex subagent bootstrap parity with pi subagents - only inject AGENTS.md and TOOLS.md
target.body:
## Summary

This PR makes Codex-native subagents follow the same workspace-bootstrap boundary introduced for OpenClaw subagents in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/56308a71447972b3913e9583d190f9038722d4b2.

That earlier change narrowed ordinary OpenClaw subagent context from:

- `AGENTS.md`
- `SOUL.md`
- `IDENTITY.md`
- `USER.md`
- `TOOLS.md`

to only:
- `AGENTS.md`
- `TOOLS.md`

The goal is correct: subagents should receive the operational/project instructions they need, but not inherit parent-session persona, identity, user-profile, memory, heartbeat, or other full persistent-session context.

However, Codex-native subagents are spawned inside the Codex extension. They do not go through the normal OpenClaw subagent bootstrap path, so the https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/56308a71447972b3913e9583d190f9038722d4b2 boundary did not apply to them. In practice, Codex-native children inherit thread-level Codex developer instructions from the parent Codex thread. If parent-only workspace files are placed there, native Codex subagents clone them too.

This PR routes Codex workspace instructions so the Codex path matches the intended subagent boundary.

## Why this is needed

OpenClaw has two different subagent execution paths:

- OpenClaw-managed subagents, which use OpenClaw's own session/bootstrap assembly.
- Codex-native subagents, which are spawned by the Codex app-server extension.

https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/56308a71447972b3913e9583d190f9038722d4b2 fixed the first path by narrowing subagent bootstrap files to `AGENTS.md` and `TOOLS.md`.

The Codex-native path needs a separate fix because Codex subagents inherit the parent Codex thread's developer instructions. Before this change, files such as `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md` could be placed in inherited thread developer instructions, which means Codex-native children could receive parent-session context that should remain parent-only.

That is both a context-boundary issue and a prompt-shaping issue:

- Subagents need tool/process guidance from `TOOLS.md`.
- Subagents should still get project guidance from Codex's native project-doc handling for `AGENTS.md`.
- Subagents should not inherit parent persona/user files such as `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, or `USER.md`.
- Parent turns still need those files, because they define the primary interactive agent's behavior and relationship to the user.

## How this solves it
This PR splits Codex workspace instruction delivery into two layers:

1. **Inherited Codex thread developer instructions**

   These are still inherited by Codex-native subagents.

   This layer now only carries `TOOLS.md`.

   `AGENTS.md` remains handled by Codex's native project-doc loading, so OpenClaw does not duplicate it into developer instructions.

2. **Turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions**

   These are attached to each parent turn through `turn/start.collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions`.
   This layer carries the parent-only workspace files:

   - `SOUL.md`
   - `IDENTITY.md`
   - `USER.md`

   Because these instructions are turn-scoped, they apply to the current parent turn without becoming part of the inherited Codex thread developer instructions that native subagents clone.

This preserves the intended behavior:

- Parent Codex turns still receive the full parent context needed for the interactive agent.
- Codex-native subagents inherit `TOOLS.md`, but not `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, or `USER.md`.

## Additional details

The PR also preserves Codex Default-mode behavior when OpenClaw adds turn-scoped developer instructions.

Codex only applies its built-in Default-mode preset when turn-scoped `developer_instructions` is `null`. Once OpenClaw adds turn-scoped workspace instructions, that field is no longer null. To avoid accidentally dropping the Default-mode collaboration guidance, this PR explicitly prepends the Default-mode instructions before the OpenClaw turn-scoped workspace overlay.

The PR also updates prompt accounting/reporting so the rendered developer-instruction size includes both:

- inherited thread developer instructions, and
- turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions.

That keeps provider-boundary prechecks, system prompt reports, trajectory records, and LLM input hooks aligned with what Codex actually receives.

## Implementation notes

Main changes:

- Split workspace developer instruction selection into inherited and turn-scoped sets.
- Keep `TOOLS.md` in inherited Codex thread developer instructions.
- Move `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, and `USER.md` into turn-scoped collaboration developer instructions.
- Preserve Codex Default-mode collaboration instructions when turn-scoped OpenClaw instructions are present.
- Add separate renderers for:
  - thread workspace instructions, using `## OpenClaw Workspace Instructions`
  - parent collaboration persona/user instructions, using `## OpenClaw Agent Soul`
- Update system prompt reporting and token/precheck accounting to include both developer-instruction layers.
- Document that Codex-native subagents follow the same `AGENTS.md` + `TOOLS.md` boundary.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: native Codex subagents inherit only stable shared workspace guidance (`AGENTS.md`/`TOOLS.md`) through thread developer instructions; persona, identity, and user workspace files stay turn-scoped so cloned Codex subagent config does not receive them by default.
- Real environment tested: Crabbox AWS run `run_41291f907e34`, lease `cbx_0a6cc82b3c29`, slug `swift-shrimp`, `openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini`, auth-profile mode, fake large `SOUL.md`/`IDENTITY.md`/`USER.md` files plus fake `AGENTS.md`/`TOOLS.md`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: ran two live Codex turns plus `/status` probes in the Crabbox setup; ran local `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check`; ran local `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.test.ts test/scripts/prompt-snapshots.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose`; ran local `pnpm test extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose -t "passes stable workspace files|uses turn-scoped collaboration instructions"`; ran `git diff --check`; ran `/Users/steipete/Projects/agent-scripts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main --engine codex --thinking low --no-web-search`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): copied live output from Crabbox terminal:

  ```text
  provider=aws lease=cbx_0a6cc82b3c29 slug=swift-shrimp run=run_41291f907e34
  model=openai-codex/gpt-5.4-mini auth_profile=openai-api-key
  fake_workspace_sizes: AGENTS=77 TOOLS=48 SOUL=57200 IDENTITY=59800 USER=57200
  turn1=OC_PROBE_ONE completed
  store_after_turn1: codex_rollout_jsonl=161129 bytes trajectory=53517 bytes
  turn2=OC_PROBE_TWO completed
  store_after_turn2: codex_rollout_jsonl=272840 bytes trajectory=160485 bytes
  marker_counts_after_two_turns_and_two_status_calls:
    SOUL_BIG_CONTEXT_MARKER=675
    IDENTITY_BIG_CONTEXT_MARKER=645
    USER_BIG_CONTEXT_MARKER=675
    OPENCLAW_TOOL_MARKER=9
  result=large persona/user files did not replay as inherited thread config; TOOLS stayed shared
  ```

  Supplemental local proof: prompt snapshots current; 46 focused tests passed plus 3 targeted run-attempt tests passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
- Observed result after fix: both live turns completed, `TOOLS.md` stayed inherited/thread-level, persona/user files stayed turn-scoped, and native Codex subagent inheritance matches the intended bootstrap boundary.
- What was not tested: full repository check suite locally.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): existing pre-fix PR proof reported a parent Codex turn and fork_context:false gpt-5.4-mini subagent both seeing actual `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `USER.md`, and `TOOLS.md` content in inherited developer context.

target.title: feat: expose rawBody on user messages in plugin hook events
target.body:
### Summary

Add `rawBody` field to user messages in `agent_end`, `before_prompt_build`, and `before_agent_start` (legacy) hook events. This provides plugins with the original user input before channel structural context, `buildInboundUserContextPrefix()` metadata, and prompt-build hook injections are prepended.

### Motivation

Plugin hooks receive `event.messages` where user message `content` includes OpenClaw-injected metadata (timestamps, channel info, sender IDs) and other hook injections (e.g., memory context). Plugins that process user content for purposes like memory extraction, summarization, or analytics must parse and strip this metadata ŌĆö a fragile approach since the format varies by channel, chat type, and OpenClaw version.

PR #51502 already established the precedent of using `RawBody` instead of `Body` to avoid passing envelope metadata to LLM processing. This PR extends that principle to plugin hook events.

### Changes

- Compute `rawBodyForHooks` from `CommandBody ?? RawBody ?? Body` in the channel message handler ŌĆö the clean user text without @mentions, sender labels, or message annotations
- Thread `rawBody` through the run params chain: `get-reply-run.ts` ŌåÆ `runReplyAgent` ŌåÆ `runAgentTurnWithFallback` ŌåÆ `runEmbeddedPiAgent` ŌåÆ `runEmbeddedAttempt`
- Include `rawBody` in hook event objects for `before_prompt_build`, `before_agent_start`, and `agent_end`
- Add `rawBody` to `FollowupRun` type so the value survives queueing/deferred runs
- `rawBody` is `undefined` for non-channel runs (CLI, heartbeat, cron)
- **Steer race fix** (`86ebda8b`): Thread `rawBody` through the steer queue path so steered injections on non-Telegram channels refresh the active embedded run's hook context

### Backward Compatibility

- Additive optional field only ŌĆö no breaking changes
- Plugins can feature-detect `rawBody` and fall back to `content`:
  ```typescript
  const userText = event.rawBody ?? extractText(lastUser.content);
  ```
- Plugin SDK API baseline unchanged (no drift)

### Use Case

Memory plugins send user+assistant pairs to backend for memory extraction. Without `rawBody`, the plugin must regex-strip injected XML blocks and cannot remove inbound metadata, causing noisy "memories" from message IDs and timestamps. With `rawBody`, the plugin gets clean user input directly.

### Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Plugin hook events (`before_prompt_build`, `agent_end`) now expose `event.rawBody` containing clean user-input text (22 chars) instead of requiring plugins to parse the metadata-decorated `messages[-1].content` (411 chars). Non-channel origins (heartbeat, cron, exec-event) correctly emit `rawBody: undefined`. Steered injections on non-Telegram channels now refresh `rawBody` via the steer queue path.

Real environment tested: Linux 6.17.0-1013-aws (arm64), Node v22.22.2, OpenClaw 2026.5.20, Bedrock `global.anthropic.claude-opus-4-7` (us-east-1, IAM role auth), Telegram bot `@oc_jmu_bot` via long-poll, custom probe plugin (`rawbody-probe`) using `definePluginEntry` + `api.on("before_prompt_build")` + `api.on("agent_end")`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built from PR HEAD (`git rev-parse HEAD` = `29e89cfb84`), ran `pnpm build`, started gateway with `node openclaw.mjs gateway --allow-unconfigured --port 18799 --bind loopback --auth none --verbose`. Sent Telegram DM "hello rawbody test 004" to `@oc_jmu_bot`. Triggered heartbeat via `node openclaw.mjs system event --text "..." --mode now --url ws://127.0.0.1:18799 --token anonymous --json`. Ran unit tests: `pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.media-only.test.ts -t "rawBody hook gating" --reporter=verbose`.

Evidence after fix:
```
Case A ŌĆö Channel happy path (Telegram DM):
[rawbody-probe] runId    = 4c1c5a9b-8e2c-405f-b6f3-d2246e4912cc
[rawbody-probe] trigger  = user
[rawbody-probe] rawBody  = "hello rawbody test 004"   (22 chars)
[rawbody-probe] lastUser = "Conversation info (untrusted metadata):\n...
                            hello rawbody test 003"   (411 chars)
[rawbody-probe] equal    = false
[rawbody-probe] eventKeys = prompt,messages,rawBody

Case B ŌĆö Heartbeat origin:
[rawbody-probe] runId    = 40d31407-6882-4f23-bdc7-85ba18fb5a41
[rawbody-probe] trigger  = heartbeat
[rawbody-probe] rawBody  = <undefined>
[rawbody-probe] lastUser = "[OpenClaw heartbeat poll]"
[rawbody-probe] eventKeys = messages,success,error,durationMs,rawBody,runId

Unit tests after fix (6/6 pass):
Ō£ō passes user text as rawBody for telegram channel runs       (62ms)
Ō£ō gates rawBody to undefined for heartbeat runs               (2ms)
Ō£ō gates rawBody to undefined for cron-event runs              (1ms)
Ō£ō gates rawBody to undefined for exec-event runs              (1ms)
Ō£ō prefers CommandBody over Body for channel rawBody           (1ms)
Ō£ō threads rawBody through steer path for non-telegram channels (3ms)
Test Files  1 passed
     Tests  6 passed
```

Observed result after fix: `event.rawBody` on `before_prompt_build` and `agent_end` contains the exact 22-char clean user input for channel runs. Non-channel origins correctly emit `undefined`. The steer path unit test confirms `followupRun.rawBody` is threaded through for non-Telegram channels (discord provider).

What was not tested: Live steer-race reproduction on Discord/Slack/WhatsApp channels (Telegram's `serializeImmediate: true` prevents triggering the steer path live). The steer fix is covered by the new unit test which mocks `isEmbeddedPiRunStreaming = true` and asserts `rawBody` propagation. No multi-user concurrency test was performed.

target.title: fix(cron): apply MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS to every-schedule jobs
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Every-schedule cron jobs did not enforce `MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS` (2s) after execution. When job execution time exceeded the interval (e.g. a 5s-interval job taking 10ŌĆō17s), the anchor-based next-run computation landed at or before `endedAt`, making the job immediately due again ŌĆö creating a tight refire loop.
- **Why it matters:** Users with short-interval `every` jobs (or jobs whose execution time exceeds the interval) experience runaway repeated execution, wasting resources and flooding logs.
- **What changed:** Removed the `if (cron) / else` branch in `applyJobResult` so `MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS` applies to **all** schedule kinds uniformly ŌĆö same safety net that cron-schedule jobs already had.
- **What did NOT change:** The `MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS` value (2s), cron-schedule behavior, error-backoff logic, `armTimer` floor delay, or any other scheduling paths.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #52097

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Every-schedule cron jobs now enforce a minimum 2-second gap between the end of one execution and the start of the next. This prevents tight refire loops when execution time exceeds the scheduled interval. Normal intervals (where execution finishes well before the next scheduled time) are unaffected.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (Docker, 32-core server)
- Runtime/container: OpenClaw v2026.3.14 in Docker
- Model/provider: N/A (systemEvent payload, no model invocation)
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: `schedule: { kind: "every", everyMs: 5000 }`

### Steps

1. Create an `every 5s` cron job: `openclaw cron add --name "bug-test" --every 5s --session main --system-event "tick"`
2. Wait for the job to fire naturally (~5s)
3. After execution completes, check `nextRunAtMs` relative to `now`

### Expected

`nextRunAtMs` should be at least 2 seconds after `endedAt` (the `MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS` safety net), preventing immediate re-firing.

### Actual (before fix)

`nextRunAtMs` equaled `endedAt` exactly (`nextRun-now: 0 sec`), causing the job to fire again immediately in a tight loop.

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

**Unit tests:** Two regression tests added in `src/cron/service/timer.applyJobResult-schedule-catch.test.ts`:
1. Daily-interval job where `lastRunAtMs + everyMs` lands exactly at `endedAt`
2. Short-interval (5s) job where execution (10s) exceeds the interval

Both tests fail without the fix (asserting `nextRunAtMs > endedAt + 1999`) and pass with it.

**Live verification on a running OpenClaw gateway (Docker):**

We reproduced the bug and verified the fix on a live OpenClaw deployment ("Bramble") running in Docker on a remote server:

1. **Bug reproduction (before fix):** Created an `every 5s` job on the live gateway running `openclaw:deploy`. The job took 17s to execute. After execution, `nextRunAtMs` was exactly equal to `endedAt` ŌĆö `nextRun-now: 0 sec`. The job was in a tight refire loop, executing back-to-back every ~17s instead of respecting the 5s interval.

2. **Fix deployment:** Cherry-picked the one-line fix onto the running branch (`fix/slack-runtime-api-docker-imports`), built a new Docker image (`openclaw:refire-fix`), and deployed it to the same gateway.

3. **Fix verification (after fix):** Created the same `every 5s` job. The job took 9.5s to execute. After execution, `nextRunAtMs = endedAt + 2000` (exactly `MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS`). The math: `nextRunAtMs (1774159583382) = lastRunAtMs (1774159571850) + lastDurationMs (9532) + MIN_REFIRE_GAP (2000)`. The tight refire loop was eliminated.

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** (1) Unit tests prove the fix for both daily-interval and short-interval edge cases. (2) Live Docker deployment reproduces the bug with the unfixed image and confirms the fix with the patched image ŌĆö same gateway, same config, same job parameters.
- **Edge cases checked:** Job execution exceeding interval (5s interval, 10ŌĆō17s execution), `lastRunAtMs + everyMs` landing exactly at `endedAt`.
- **What you did not verify:** "at" (one-shot) schedule behavior (unchanged code path), interaction with `preserveSchedule` on branches that have it, or cron-schedule behavior (already had `MIN_REFIRE_GAP`).

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert the single commit; the change is a 3-line removal of an `if/else` branch.
- Files/config to restore: `src/cron/service/timer.ts` ŌĆö restore the `if (job.schedule.kind === "cron")` / `else` branch.
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Every-schedule jobs not firing at all (would indicate `MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS` somehow exceeds the interval, but at 2s this is only possible with sub-2s intervals).

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Jobs with very short intervals (<2s) will now fire at most every 2 seconds instead of their configured interval.
  - Mitigation: `MIN_REFIRE_GAP_MS` is 2 seconds ŌĆö a reasonable floor. Sub-2s cron intervals are uncommon and would create high load regardless. The same floor already applies to cron-schedule jobs.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(webchat): stabilize live transcript run state
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #83528 and #82611; refs #83949.

- Mirror Codex app-server inbound prompts at turn start with the existing `${turnId}:prompt` identity so WebChat can see external-channel user messages before the turn finishes, while preserving `suppressNextUserMessagePersistence` for already-persisted inbound prompts.
- Keep hidden external-channel live runs private by sending chat/tool/agent updates only to exact selected-session message subscribers instead of broadcasting globally.
- Subscribe Control UI to the selected session message stream on connect/reconnect and session switch, adopt observed run ids for non-WebChat-originated runs including the default main alias/canonical key pair, dedupe accumulated stream snapshots around tool cards, and keep selected-session `session.message` reloads alias-aware.
- Emit stable `messageSeq` values for multi-message Codex mirror batches so selected-session subscribers receive per-message transcript positions instead of post-batch duplicates.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: WebChat/Control UI now has durable inbound prompt truth at Codex turn start, selected-session live updates for external-channel runs without unrelated-session leakage, passive run-id adoption and `session.message` handling across the default main alias/canonical key pair, reconnect-safe selected-session subscription state, stable mirrored transcript message sequences, suppression-safe prompt mirroring, and no duplicated accumulated stream text around tool cards.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw Codex worktree on branch `meow/webchat-transcript-run-state-truth`, latest head `e19523ba6c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- -t "does not mirror the Codex prompt early when user message persistence is suppressed"`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/transcript-mirror.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
  - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
  - `node --import tsx scripts/check-no-extension-test-core-imports.ts`
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- Evidence after fix:
  - Suppression regression: failed before the guard because the forbidden prompt appeared; passed after the guard.
  - Codex app-server tests: 2 files passed, 233 tests passed.
  - Gateway/session event tests: 4 files passed, 111 tests passed.
  - Control UI tests: 7 files passed, 238 tests passed.
  - Rebased-base test-type command passed.
  - Boundary check passed: `check-no-extension-test-core-imports` checked 1915 extension files and 1 plugin helper.
  - `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` passed on latest head.
  - The regressions prove message-bearing prompt transcript updates with message ids and stable sequences, suppressed prompt persistence does not early-mirror a duplicate user row, multi-message mirror batch `messageSeq` `[1, 2]`, selected-session sequence delivery, alias/canonical subscription de-dupe, stale subscription completion cleanup, main-alias `session.message` reloads, and passive main-alias run-id adoption.
- Observed result after fix: The early Codex prompt mirror writes one user transcript row and emits a message-bearing selected-session update before assistant completion only when prompt persistence is not suppressed; final mirrored batches emit correct per-message sequence metadata; hidden external-channel chat events route only to exact selected-session subscribers; selected Control UI sessions subscribe/re-subscribe without alias churn or stale async overwrite; canonical `agent:main:main` transcript events reload selected `main`; passive chat state adopts observed run ids; and accumulated stream snapshots render only their new suffix after tool cards.
- What was not tested: Live Feishu or Telegram external-channel browser proof was not run in this pass. Remote `pnpm check:changed` through Crabbox was attempted before this follow-up but blocked: Blacksmith Testbox is not configured for `.github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`, and default AWS Crabbox could not refresh credentials because no EC2 IMDS role/credentials were available on this machine.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -- -t "does not mirror the Codex prompt early when user message persistence is suppressed"`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/transcript-mirror.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-chat.agent-events.test.ts src/gateway/session-message-events.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/chat.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/sessions.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/build-chat-items.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway.sessions.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render.helpers.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-gateway-chat-load.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-settings.refresh-active-tab.node.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo`
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-no-extension-test-core-imports.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`

target.title: feat(mcp): channel-mediated approval for MCP tool calls (consent envelope)
target.body:
Closes #78308

## Summary

Today, when a bundle-MCP tool returns to the agent, OpenClaw passes the result straight through to the model ŌĆö there's no approval gate analogous to the one [`exec-approvals`](../tools/exec-approvals.md) provides for shell commands. For act-tier MCP tools (send email, create vault entry, set HA state, etc.), the only thing standing between the user and the action is *the model deciding to call it*.

This PR adds a small, additive contract ŌĆö the **MCP consent envelope** ŌĆö that lets MCP servers opt into the existing `/approve <id> allow-once|allow-always|deny` pipeline that already backs shell-exec approvals. **Servers that don't opt in are unaffected.**

## The contract

When a server wants user approval for a call, it returns:

```json
{
  "ok": false,
  "requires_confirmation": true,
  "action_id": "<server-side single-use token>",
  "summary": "<one-line description of the action>",
  "expires_in_seconds": 60
}
```

ŌĆ”in either `structuredContent` or as JSON inside `content[0].text`. OpenClaw recognises the envelope, suppresses the result, and:

1. Issues a `plugin.approval.request` to the local gateway (reuses everything: `channel-approval-auth`, ID-prefix routing, the magic-word reply parser).
2. Blocks until the user replies on the trusted channel.
3. On `allow-once` / `allow-always`: re-calls the tool with `confirmation_token = action_id` set on the input. Returns that second result to the agent.
4. On `deny` / expiry / error: returns a synthetic `{ok:false, approved:false, reason}` result. The model never sees `action_id`.

## Why the trust boundary actually moves

The model never sees `action_id` and **`confirmation_token` is stripped from any model-supplied input** on the first call. Even if a malicious or careless agent fabricates a token, the MCP server's redemption check rejects it (the server is responsible for one-shot/TTL enforcement of `action_id`).

This is the same pattern OpenClaw already enforces for shell exec via `exec-approvals` ŌĆö *agent asks, user authorises, runtime executes*. The PR just generalises that pattern to MCP tools that opt into the contract.

## What changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-consent.ts` | **New.** Envelope detection, the default approval requester (calls `plugin.approval.request` + `waitDecision`), `confirmation_token` scrubber, denied-result builder, **`sanitiseToolEmittedApprovalText`** (review-comment defence ŌĆö neutralises `/approve` in MCP-emitted text). ~270 lines. |
| `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-materialize.ts` | Wraps every tool's `execute()` in a new `callMcpToolWithConsent` orchestrator. Plumbs `requestApproval`, `consentEnabled`, **`agentId`, and `sessionKey`** through `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun` and `createBundleMcpToolRuntime`. |
| `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts`, `compact.ts` | Pass the agent harness's existing `agentId` + `sessionKey` into the materialize layer so the gateway forwarder can resolve the user's actual delivery channel (WhatsApp / Telegram / Slack / web UI). |
| `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-approve.ts` | Accept bare `/approve allow-once` (no id) when there's exactly one pending approval ŌĆö typing a uuid on a phone is unrealistic UX. On ambiguity (>1 pending), refuses with a hint including the explicit form. |
| `src/infra/plugin-approvals.ts` | Cleaner reply prompt: shows all three decisions (`allow-once \| allow-always \| deny`) inline, copy-paste-ready, with the id on its own line for the explicit fallback. |
| `src/config/types.mcp.ts` | Adds `mcp.approvals.enabled?: boolean` (default true). |
| `src/agents/pi-bundle-mcp-consent.test.ts` | **New.** 21 unit tests including a live-caught `decision: null` regression and a `agentId/sessionKey` propagation regression. |
| `docs/tools/mcp-consent-envelope.md` | **New.** Server-author contract, configuration, trust-boundary explanation. |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Entry under `Unreleased`. |

No new approval kind is introduced ŌĆö the implementation **reuses the existing `"plugin"` ApprovalKind**.

## Test plan

- Ō£ģ `pnpm install`
- Ō£ģ `pnpm lint` ŌĆö 8234 files, 213 rules, 0 warnings, 0 errors.
- Ō£ģ Full agents-core suite: **272 files passed (272), 3515 tests passed | 4 skipped**.
- Ō£ģ `pi-bundle-mcp-consent.test.ts` ŌĆö 21/21 green.
- Ō£ģ `commands-approve.test.ts` ŌĆö 18/18 green (existing tests still pass after the implicit-id parser change).

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Without channel-mediated approval, the model is the trust gate for MCP tool calls that want user approval. Captured live on **vanilla `OpenClaw 2026.4.24`** with HomeBrain's MCP servers: when the agent fired `homebrain-nextcloud__nc-files_share`, the consent envelope (with `action_id`) passed straight to the model, which **leaked the token verbatim into chat**, **offered to self-confirm**, and **hallucinated a second action_id off-by-one** when it lost track of the first. Full screenshot + analysis: [#78308 comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78308#issuecomment-4391533882). This PR replaces that prompt-level honour-system with runtime-enforced channel approval.

**Environment tested:** `homebrain@192.168.178.58`. x86_64 Ubuntu 24.04. AMD Radeon RX 9060 XT (16 GB VRAM). llama.cpp + Qwen3.6-35B-A3B (Q5_K_XL). HomeBrain reference MCP servers from [oalterg/HomeBrain](https://github.com/oalterg/HomeBrain). **OpenClaw built directly from this PR branch and installed live** in place of the npm-shipped 2026.4.24 binary for the runtime-side tests.

**Steps run after the patch:**

```bash
// Local: build + pack the PR branch
pnpm build && npm pack
// Deploy the .tgz to the target box
scp openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz homebrain@192.168.178.58:/tmp/
ssh homebrain@192.168.178.58 'echo admin | sudo -S bash -c "
  cd /usr/lib/node_modules
  rm -rf openclaw && mkdir openclaw && cd openclaw
  tar xzf /tmp/openclaw-2026.5.6.tgz --strip-components=1
  npm install --omit=dev --no-audit --no-fund --silent"'
// Set the missing approvals.plugin config (the forwarder needs it to deliver)
ssh homebrain@192.168.178.58 'sudo -u homebrain jq ".approvals = {plugin:{enabled:true,mode:\"session\"}}" \
  ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json > /tmp/c && sudo -u homebrain cp /tmp/c ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json'
ssh homebrain@192.168.178.58 'sudo -u homebrain systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway'

// User sends on WhatsApp:
//   "Create a public share link to /uploads in Nextcloud, expires in 7 days."
// OpenClaw routes through the agent harness, which fires nc.files_share.
// My code intercepts the consent envelope, calls plugin.approval.request.
// The forwarder delivers the prompt to the user's WhatsApp + web UI.
// User taps Allow once on the web UI (or replies /approve allow-once on WhatsApp).
// Runtime re-calls the tool with confirmation_token. Real share URL returns.
```

**Evidence after fix:**

The web UI's native plugin-approval modal renders my PR's `plugin.approval.request` exactly right ŌĆö title `MCP tool approval`, description `homebrain-nextcloud.nc.files_share ŌĆö Nextcloud: create public share link for /uploads (expires in 7 days)`, severity `warning`, plugin `mcp:homebrain-nextcloud`, three buttons `Allow once / Always allow / Deny`, expiry countdown. The user pressed `Allow once`. Screenshot captured (not committed; happy to attach for maintainer review). Same approval was offered on the user's WhatsApp simultaneously via the routing-fix commit.

```text
$ openclaw --version  # the deployed PR build
OpenClaw 2026.5.6 (a2cd999)

// Gateway log (verbatim) at the moment the user tapped Allow once:
2026-05-06T20:01:22 Ōćä res Ō£ō plugin.approval.waitDecision 59412ms
                    conn=6f5914caŌĆ”dfe2 id=0c6f887aŌĆ”7e6d

// /var/log/homebrain/mcp-nextcloud-audit.log ŌĆö the result of the consent loop:
2026-05-06T20:01:22 {"server":"nextcloud","action":"share",
                     "path":"/uploads","expire_days":7}

// Nextcloud confirms the actual share resource exists on disk:
$ curl -u admin:<app-pass> -H 'OCS-APIRequest: true' \
  http://127.0.0.1:8080/ocs/v2.php/apps/files_sharing/api/v1/shares
{"id":"2","share_type":3,"path":"/uploads","stime":1778097682,
 "url":"http://127.0.0.1:8080/s/iiaRr54CKLnZ7sC", ...}
```

**Observed result after the fix:**

- **Vanilla baseline (without PR):** the model leaks `action_id` to chat, offers to self-confirm, hallucinates a second action_id off-by-one. Trust boundary is the model's behavior ŌĆö soft gate. [Screenshot + analysis in #78308 comment](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78308#issuecomment-4391533882).
- **PR build:** the agent fires the tool, the consent envelope is intercepted, `plugin.approval.request` is issued to the gateway with the correct `agentId` + `sessionKey` so the forwarder can route. Approval prompt arrives in the **web UI's native modal** (gorgeous render, all three decisions one tap) AND on the user's **WhatsApp** simultaneously. User taps `Allow once` (or replies `/approve allow-once`). Runtime re-calls the tool with `confirmation_token = action_id`. **Real Nextcloud share is created** ŌĆö verified by the audit log entry, the gateway `waitDecision 59412ms Ō£ō` line, and the actual `share` row in NC's `files_sharing` API.
- **A live regression bug was caught and fixed during this very run**: `decision: null` from the gateway in two-phase mode means "pending, call waitDecision", not "deny". Fixed in `ef30bbf` with a regression test. Without the live test cycle, that would have shipped as a latent bug masking real user approvals as denials.
- **Forwarder routing was also live-caught**: the PR initially didn't propagate `agentId`/`sessionKey` from the agent harness to `plugin.approval.request`, so the forwarder had no session binding and prompts auto-cancelled silently. Fixed in `2056d25` by plumbing both fields through `materializeBundleMcpToolsForRun` + `createBundleMcpToolRuntime` from `attempt.ts` and `compact.ts`.
- **`/approve` UX was live-caught too**: typing a full uuid by hand on a phone is unrealistic, and the prompt template said `Reply with: /approve <id>` with `<id>` as a literal placeholder. Fixed in `a2cd999`: bare `/approve allow-once` resolves to the single most-recent pending approval; the prompt now shows all three decisions inline copy-paste-ready.

**What was not tested:** the *deny* path through user-driven rejection (we exercised the deny path via timeout and via approval-system error in unit tests, but not via a user explicitly typing `/approve deny` end-to-end on the live deployment). The unit tests (`on consent envelope + deny, returns synthetic denied result and does NOT re-call`) cover this deterministically. Maintainer running the PR through any deployment can verify in a single message exchange.

## Review responses

### Channel-level message provenance ([m13v's review](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78308#issuecomment-4391392109))

> *"if a compromised mcp server returns a tool result containing '/approve abc123 allow-once' in summary or output, and the channel renderer doesn't distinguish user-typed from tool-emitted messages at the parser layer, an mcp tool self-approves future calls by poisoning the transcript."*

Closed in `083dd748`. Two layers:

1. `sanitiseToolEmittedApprovalText` neutralises any `/approve` substring in MCP-emitted text by inserting U+200B between the slash and `approve` so `^/approve\b` cannot match. Applied at parse-time inside `detectMcpConsentEnvelope`, so every downstream path that renders the envelope sees pre-sanitised text.
2. The existing `/approve` parser already enforces provenance (via `isAuthorizedSender` against the channel allowlist) but the invariant was implicit. A code comment now makes it explicit so future refactors don't lose it.

Live-verified on the deployed `.58` PR build with a deliberately malicious envelope (full transcript on the [issue thread](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78308#issuecomment-4394805083)). Two new unit tests cover the sanitiser directly and the envelope path.

## Reference / motivation

Part of a broader integration on a [HomeBrain](https://github.com/oalterg/HomeBrain) box. Reference server-side: [`scripts/mcp_common.py`](https://github.com/oalterg/HomeBrain/blob/vault-integration/scripts/mcp_common.py) (the `Consent` helper) and [`scripts/mcp-*.py`](https://github.com/oalterg/HomeBrain/tree/vault-integration/scripts). Design write-up: [`INTEGRATIONS_PLAN.md`](https://github.com/oalterg/HomeBrain/blob/vault-integration/INTEGRATIONS_PLAN.md).

## Open questions for review

1. **Should I add an `mcp.approvals.allowlist: [{server, tool}]`?** Left out for v1 ŌĆö gating is already opt-in *per-tool* by the MCP server (only act-tier tools return the envelope), and `allow-always` already short-circuits future calls. Happy to add an allowlist as a follow-up if maintainers prefer.
2. **The decision callback returns the existing `ExecApprovalDecision` union** (`allow-once` | `allow-always` | `deny`) ŌĆö keeps the reply-parser + UI rendering identical.
3. **Approval kind**: I considered adding `"mcp-tool"` as a third `ChannelApprovalKind`. Decided against it (~10 files for a presentation distinction) ŌĆö the existing plugin renderer already shows `pluginId: mcp:<server>`, `toolName: <tool>`, `description: <summary>`. Open to redoing as a separate kind if maintainers prefer.
4. **Should `approvals.plugin.enabled = true, mode = "session"` be the default?** Currently the gateway's plugin-approval forwarder is fully gated on this config block being explicitly set. Without it set, requests are accepted but never delivered ŌĆö the boundary becomes a permanent deny gate (caught live during this PR's testing). Sensible default seems to be on-with-session-routing, especially now that the contract is broadly useful for MCP tool calls. Happy to add that as a separate PR if maintainers agree.

­¤ż¢ Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(gateway): add provider auth prewarm operator controls
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: provider auth prewarm can still be expensive during gateway startup or recovery, and affected operators do not have a supported way to disable or delay it.
- Solution: expose a gateway-level provider auth prewarm policy with config and env controls.
- What changed: added `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled`, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.delayMs`, and `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM`; startup, reload, logout-triggered rewarm, delayed startup prewarm, and auth-profile-failure rewarm paths now honor the resolved policy.
- Docs: documented the new config keys and env override in `docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md`, and updated the generated config baseline hash.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): this does not claim to fix the underlying event-loop starvation in `warmCurrentProviderAuthState`, Discord READY timing, or OpenAI/Codex route fallback behavior.

## Motivation

This PR follows up on the Discord/operator recovery report in #85997. It also relates to the narrower provider-auth startup/prewarm reproduction in #85999.

This proposes an operator safety valve while maintainers evaluate the deeper scheduling/caching fix for provider auth prewarm. It is intentionally not marked as closing either issue because the user-facing reports cover broader startup, channel readiness, and auth-provider routing behavior.

AI-assisted: drafted and implemented with Codex; human review and maintainer product feedback are requested before merge.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [x] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Follow-up to #85997.
- Related to #85999.
- This PR does not close either issue; it proposes a scoped mitigation for provider auth prewarm operator control.
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: affected deployments can now disable provider auth prewarm or increase its startup delay without patching OpenClaw internals.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Ubuntu Linux server. Startup proof was captured on this branch before the final sidecar-policy repair; the branch is now rebased on `origin/main` at `483d7be6c4` with current head `3f3fb0361c`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Started a real gateway process from this checkout with an isolated temp `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`.
  - Used `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_STARTUP_TRACE=1` to keep the run hermetic while still exercising the gateway startup/post-ready path.
  - Ran three redacted startup cases: default config, `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`, and config-enabled plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
- Evidence after fix (redacted terminal/log proof):

Default config still schedules and runs provider auth prewarm:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=1.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] provider auth state pre-warmed in 75ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] default-enabled elapsedMs=16851 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Config disable skips the provider auth prewarm sidecar:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.5ms total=5670.5ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] config-disabled elapsedMs=10409 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

Env override skips prewarm even when config enables it:

```text
[gateway] startup trace: sidecars.ready loadedPluginCount=0.0 postReadySidecarCount=0.0
[gateway] ready
[gateway] startup trace: post-ready.maintenance 13.3ms total=5482.6ms eventLoopMax=0.0ms
[proof] env-disabled elapsedMs=10152 port=<redacted> stateDir=<redacted>
```

- Targeted verification:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts` -> 3 files passed, 88 tests passed after final rebase.
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/config/schema.help.quality.test.ts` -> 2 files passed, 111 tests passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm config:docs:check` -> passed after final rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core` -> passed after final rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:core:test` -> passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
  - `corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test` -> passed before the final no-conflict rebase.
- Observed result after fix: default behavior remains enabled; config/env disable paths skip startup prewarm; delayed startup prewarm and auth-profile-failure rewarm re-check the current policy before running; reload and logout rewarm paths skip when the same policy disables provider auth prewarm.
- What was not tested: live Discord restart timing, real provider CLI starvation on a constrained VM, and OpenAI Codex OAuth fallback behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #85999 includes startup prewarm timing logs; #85997 includes redacted Discord/operator-visible errors.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: current main already defers provider auth prewarm, but the exposed prewarm controls are internal test/runtime options rather than an operator-facing config surface.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no schema-covered config/env option to disable or delay provider auth prewarm on deployments where the prewarm path harms startup or channel recovery.
- Contributing context (if known): #85272 narrowed startup ordering by deferring prewarm; #85999 reports that provider auth prewarm can still monopolize startup on a constrained host.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-reload-handlers.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/models-auth-status.test.ts`, `src/config/config-misc.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: disabled prewarm policy skips startup/reload/logout rewarm paths, env override disables config-enabled prewarm, configured delay is honored, and a long-lived provider-auth sidecar re-checks current config before delayed startup or auth-profile-failure rewarm.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the change is policy resolution and call-site gating; these tests cover each touched call path without requiring live providers.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): startup prewarm ordering coverage existed, but not operator-facing config/env policy.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Adds optional gateway config and env controls:

```json
{
  "gateway": {
    "providerAuthPrewarm": {
      "enabled": false,
      "delayMs": 5000
    }
  }
}
```

`OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1` disables provider auth prewarm regardless of config.

Defaults remain unchanged: provider auth prewarm stays enabled with the existing startup delay.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [provider auth prewarm always scheduled]

After:
[gateway startup/reload/logout] -> [resolve config/env policy] -> [schedule or skip provider auth prewarm]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`No`)
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`)
- New/changed network calls? (`No`)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`)
- Data access scope changed? (`No`)
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Ubuntu Linux server
- Runtime/container: local Node/pnpm checkout
- Model/provider: provider behavior mocked in tests; real gateway startup proof used isolated temp config/state
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway startup/reload/logout paths; no live Discord run
- Relevant config (redacted): synthetic gateway config with `auth.mode: none`, `controlUi.enabled: false`, temporary port, and no real channel/provider credentials

### Steps

1. Start gateway with default synthetic config and startup trace enabled.
2. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: false`.
3. Start gateway with `gateway.providerAuthPrewarm.enabled: true` plus `OPENCLAW_SKIP_PROVIDER_AUTH_PREWARM=1`.
4. Run targeted gateway/config tests, config docs check, and core typecheck.

### Expected

- Default policy schedules and runs provider auth prewarm.
- Disabled policy skips provider auth prewarm.
- Env override wins over config-enabled prewarm.
- Configured delay controls startup prewarm delay.
- Long-lived sidecar rewarm paths respect the current config after hot reload.

### Actual

- Matches expected in redacted real gateway startup logs, targeted tests, local typechecks, and docs check.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real gateway startup proof for default/config-disabled/env-disabled behavior; targeted gateway/config tests; docs baseline check; `tsgo:core`; `tsgo:core:test`; `tsgo:extensions:test`.
- Edge cases checked: env override disabling config-enabled prewarm; invalid schema values rejected; reload/logout rewarm paths honor the same policy; long-lived sidecar skips delayed startup and auth-profile-failure rewarm after current config disables prewarm.
- What you did **not** verify: live Discord startup recovery, real constrained-host prewarm timing, and provider OAuth fallback behavior.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`Yes`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: no migration required; operators may opt into the new config/env controls.

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: disabling prewarm may make model/provider auth status colder until first use.
  - Mitigation: default behavior remains enabled; the disable path is explicit opt-in for affected operators.
- Risk: this may be considered a product/config surface decision rather than the preferred root fix.
  - Mitigation: scoped as a mitigation, and does not close the linked issues.

target.title: cron: reject webhook URLs with embedded credentials
target.body: ## Summary Describe the problem and fix in 2ŌĆō5 bullets: - Problem: Cron webhook URLs could include embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:pass@example.com/hook), which can leak secrets in logs, config, or URLs. - Why it matters: URL-embedded credentials are discouraged and can expose secrets; using query params or headers is preferred. - What changed: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl now rejects URLs with username or password in the parsed URL. - What did NOT change (scope boundary): No changes to auth headers, query params, or other credential handling; only http(s)://user:pass@host URLs are rejected. ## Change Type (select all) - [ ] Bug fix - [ ] Feature - [ ] Refactor required for the fix - [ ] Docs - [x] Security hardening - [ ] Chore/infra ## Scope (select all touched areas) - [x] Gateway / orchestration - [ ] Skills / tool execution - [ ] Auth / tokens - [ ] Memory / storage - [ ] Integrations - [ ] API / contracts - [ ] UI / DX - [ ] CI/CD / infra ## Linked Issue/PR - Closes # - Related # ## User-visible / Behavior Changes Cron webhook URLs with embedded credentials (e.g. https://user:secret@host/path) are now rejected as invalid. URLs without credentials continue to work. ## Security Impact (required) - New permissions/capabilities? (`No`) - Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`No`) - New/changed network calls? (`No`) - Command/tool execution surface changed? (`No`) - Data access scope changed? (`No`) - If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: ## Repro + Verification ### Environment - OS: macOS (darwin) - Runtime/container: Node 22+ - Model/provider: N/A - Integration/channel (if any): CRON webhook delivery - Relevant config (redacted): N/A ### Steps 1. Add or update a cron job with webhook delivery using a URL with embedded credentials, e.g. https://user:pass@example.invalid/webhook. 2. Observe that the webhook URL is rejected (job will not use it). 3. Use the same job with https://example.invalid/webhook (no credentials) and confirm it is accepted. ### Expected - URLs with user:pass@ in the authority are rejected. - URLs without embedded credentials continue to work. ### Actual - Behavior matches above ## Evidence Attach at least one: - [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after - [x] Trace/log snippets - [ ] Screenshot/recording - [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant) ## Human Verification (required) What you personally verified (not just CI), and how: - Verified scenarios: normalizeHttpWebhookUrl returns null for URLs like http://user:pass@host/path; returns the URL for http://host/path. - Edge cases checked: URLs with only username (user@host), only password (not typical in URLs), and no credentials. - What you did **not** verify: End-to-end cron job creation via CLI or UI with credential URLs; live webhook delivery. ## Review Conversations - [ ] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR. - [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment. If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers. ## Compatibility / Migration - Backward compatible? (`No`) - Config/env changes? (`No`) - Migration needed? (`Yes`) - If yes, exact upgrade steps: Update any cron job webhook URLs that include user:pass@ in the URL. Remove embedded credentials and use headers or query params for auth instead. ## Failure Recovery (if this breaks) - How to disable/revert this change quickly: Revert this commit. - Files/config to restore: src/cron/webhook-url.ts - Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for: Jobs with credential URLs will fail validation or treat the webhook as invalid. ## Risks and Mitigations List only real risks for this PR. Add/remove entries as needed. If none, write `None`. - Risk: Existing jobs using credential URLs will break. - Mitigation: Document in release notes; credential URLs are discouraged; alternative auth methods should be used.

target.title: fix(install): add pipe guard to prevent stdin stealing in curl | bash
target.body:
## Problem

When invoked via `curl -fsSL https://openclaw.ai/install.sh | bash`, bash reads the script incrementally from stdin. Interactive prompts (gum, read) and child processes that also read stdin steal bytes from the script stream, causing:

1. Truncated function names: `warn_shell_path_missing_di` instead of `warn_shell_path_missing_dir`
2. "command not found" errors for the truncated names
3. Indefinite hangs (the script stream is corrupted, bash blocks waiting for more input)

Reported on WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 with 3/3 reproduction rate.

## Fix

Add a pipe guard at the top of `install.sh` that detects piped execution via empty `BASH_SOURCE[0]` (piped scripts have no source file), buffers the remaining stdin to a temp file using `cat`, and re-executes from the file via `exec`. This frees stdin for interactive prompts and child processes.

The guard:
- **Does not fire** for direct execution (`bash install.sh`) or process substitution (`bash <(curl ...)`) because `BASH_SOURCE` is set
- **Does not fire** for CI/Docker with non-tty stdin when running from a file
- **Cleans up** the temp file immediately after re-execution starts
- Uses `_OPENCLAW_PIPE_BUFFERED` env var to prevent infinite re-execution loops

Fixes #73814

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: the installer now buffers itself to a temp file when piped via curl, preventing child processes from consuming stdin bytes that corrupt the script stream.
Real environment tested: Linux x86_64, OpenClaw 2026.5.12 installed, Node v26.0.0, bash 5.2. Tested both piped execution (`cat install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method npm`) and direct execution (`bash install.sh --install-method npm < /dev/null`).
Exact steps or command run after this patch: (1) verified pipe guard pattern with a minimal test script that includes a deliberate stdin steal (`head -c 50 < /dev/stdin`); (2) ran full installer in piped mode; (3) ran full installer in direct mode with non-tty stdin; (4) confirmed both complete successfully without truncation.
Evidence after fix: terminal output copied below.

Pipe guard pattern verification (minimal test script with deliberate stdin steal):
```
$ cat /tmp/pipe-test-steal.sh | bash
Before stdin steal
After stdin steal
Function executed correctly
Script completed
```
Without the guard, the `head -c 50` would consume 50 bytes of the script stream, truncating the function definition. With the guard, the script runs from a temp file so stdin stealing has no effect.

Full installer in piped mode (script runs from temp file):
```
$ cat scripts/install.sh | bash -s -- --install-method npm 2>&1 | tail -10
Ō£ō OpenClaw installed

[3/3] Finalizing setup
┬Ę Running doctor to migrate settings
┬Ę Running doctor
Ō£ō Doctor complete

­¤”× OpenClaw installed successfully (2026.5.12)!
```
Note: error source shows `/tmp/tmp.uuj4JcpyzQ: line 2988` confirming the guard buffered to temp file.

Full installer in direct mode (BASH_SOURCE is set, guard does not fire):
```
$ bash scripts/install.sh --install-method npm < /dev/null 2>&1 | tail -10
Ō£ō Doctor complete

­¤”× OpenClaw installed successfully (2026.5.12)!
```
Note: error source shows `scripts/install.sh: line 3000` confirming guard did not fire.

Observed result after fix: piped execution completes successfully without truncated function names or hangs. Direct execution is unaffected. The pipe guard adds negligible overhead (one mktemp + cat + exec).
What was not tested: macOS bash 3.2 (BASH_SOURCE behavior may differ in very old bash versions). WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 (the reporter's environment) was not tested directly, but the fix is bash-level and platform-independent.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` ŌåÆ `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- **Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner ŌĆö the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- **Evidence after fix**: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function ŌĆö bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- **Observed result after fix**: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function ŌĆö pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check ŌĆö has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key ŌĆö so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` ŌåÆ `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- **What was not tested**: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` ŌĆö 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits ŌĆö the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) ŌĆö the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** ŌĆö the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token ŌåÆ mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   ŌåÆ mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity ŌĆö it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
